householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied greatly. Gentry and prosperous merchants could hold land, rental income, cash reserves, and silver plate; ordinary peasant households mainly possessed land-use rights, livestock, tools, and grain stores, with limited resilience against hardship

economicSystem.zh: 以封建土地关系为基础、但日益货币化和市场化的混合经济；王室通过关税、特许公司、港口监管和对羊毛-呢绒出口的控制参与经济治理
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on feudal land relations but increasingly monetized and market-oriented; the crown participated in economic governance through customs, chartered companies, port regulation, and oversight of the wool-cloth export trade

source.zh: 封建权利、监护权与婚姻税
source.en: Feudal dues, wardships, and marriage rights

source.zh: 司法罚金、债券与担保金
议会非常税与其他杂项

category.zh: 债务清偿、赏赐与杂项储备
category.en: Debt repayment, patronage, and miscellaneous reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 到亨利七世晚期，王室储备显著优于内战时代。国王通过恢复王室地产、谨慎支出、严密审计与债券制度积累了可观现金与贵金属储备，但部分资产并非完全流动且带有政治强制色彩
treasuryDescription.en: By the later part of Henry VII's reign, crown reserves were markedly stronger than in the civil-war era. Through restoring crown lands, careful spending, strict audit, and the bond system, the king accumulated substantial cash and bullion reserves, though part of these assets was not fully liquid and carried a coercive political character

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策总体保守、积累导向且强调王权独立财源。亨利七世力图以常规收入、海关、罚金与封建权利维持财政稳健，避免频繁向议会求税，同时也因征敛严苛而招致不满
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally conservative, accumulation-oriented, and focused on independent royal income. Henry VII sought to preserve solvency through ordinary revenues, customs, fines, and feudal rights while avoiding frequent parliamentary taxation, though the rigor of collection also bred resentment

description.zh: 规模不大，主要负责宫廷警卫、王室安全和战时核心指挥护卫
description.en: Small in number, mainly responsible for court security, royal protection, and a core wartime command guard

description.zh: 战时依靠郡级征召和贵族、乡绅动员，是英格兰军力主体，但训练和装备水平差异较大
description.en: Raised in wartime through county musters and noble and gentry mobilization, these formed the bulk of English manpower, though training and equipment varied widely

description.zh: 长弓传统仍具象征与战术价值，配合长柄武器步兵构成英军主要野战兵种
description.en: The longbow tradition still retained symbolic and tactical value, combining with polearm infantry as the main field arm of English forces

description.zh: 尚非后世常设皇家海军，但王室已开始重视造船与舰只储备，可在需要时动员商船和少量王室舰船执行巡防与护航
description.en: Not yet the permanent Royal Navy of later centuries, but the crown had begun to invest in shipbuilding and naval reserves, enabling it to mobilize merchant shipping and a small number of royal vessels for patrol and escort

commanderInChief.zh: 亨利七世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Henry VII

technology.zh: 军事技术处于中世纪晚期向早期火器时代过渡阶段，继续使用长弓、甲胄骑兵、长柄兵器，并逐步采用火绳枪前身、手铳和攻城火炮；港防与舰船火炮仍在发展中
technology.en: Military technology was in transition from the late medieval to the early gunpowder age, continuing to use longbows, armored cavalry, and polearms while gradually adopting early handguns and siege artillery; coastal defenses and shipboard artillery were still developing

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自苏格兰边境、与法国关系反复引发的海峡对峙、约克派流亡者和僭称者的外援阴谋，以及爱尔兰地区的不稳定
threats.en: Main threats came from the Scottish border, recurring cross-Channel tensions linked to France, Yorkist exiles and pretenders backed from abroad, and instability in Ireland

populationDescription.zh: 1500年前后的英格兰人口仍处于黑死病后长期恢复阶段，规模明显低于14世纪初峰值，但已重现增长。人口分布以乡村为主，东南部与中部农业区较稠密，北部和边地较稀疏
populationDescription.en: Around 1500 England's population was still in a long post-Black Death recovery, clearly below the early fourteenth-century peak but growing again. Settlement remained predominantly rural, with denser populations in the southeast and Midlands and thinner settlement in the north and borderlands

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与高等贵族、主教和修道院领袖、骑士与乡绅、城市商人和行会工匠、自由农与佃农、雇农与无地劳工，以及依赖教区和施舍的贫民
socialClasses.en: Major social groups included the monarchy and high nobility, bishops and monastic leaders, knights and gentry, urban merchants and guild craftsmen, free peasants and tenants, hired farm laborers and the landless, and the poor dependent on parish and charitable support

allies.zh: 英格兰在1500年前后没有持久固定盟友，但通过与西班牙的婚姻联盟、与勃艮第-低地国家的商业安排，以及阶段性与神圣罗马帝国和阿拉贡的合作维持均势
allies.en: Around 1500 England had no permanent fixed allies, but maintained balance through a marriage alliance with Spain, commercial arrangements with Burgundy-the Low Countries, and periodic cooperation with the Holy Roman Empire and Aragon

enemies.zh: 主要对手是苏格兰王国与法国王国；此外，支持珀金·沃贝克等僭称者的外国宫廷也被视为潜在敌对力量
enemies.en: Its principal rivals were the Kingdom of Scotland and the Kingdom of France; foreign courts backing pretenders such as Perkin Warbeck were also regarded as hostile forces

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以王朝安全、贸易利益和欧陆均势为核心。亨利七世偏好谨慎现实主义，通过婚姻、条约、有限战争、贸易禁运与外交斡旋确保都铎王朝合法性并避免代价高昂的大陆冒险
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on dynastic security, commercial interests, and the continental balance of power. Henry VII favored cautious realism, using marriage, treaties, limited war, trade embargoes, and diplomacy to secure Tudor legitimacy while avoiding costly continental adventures

era.zh: 文艺复兴早期与晚期中世纪向近代早期过渡阶段
era.en: Early Renaissance and the transitional phase from the late medieval to the early modern world

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与制度进展包括印刷术在英格兰的扩散、会计与档案管理改进、远洋与沿海航运技术提升、火药武器逐步普及，以及毛纺生产和城市商业金融工具的持续发展
keyInnovations.en: Key technological and institutional developments included the spread of printing in England, improved accounting and record administration, advances in seagoing and coastal shipping, gradual diffusion of gunpowder weapons, and continued development of woolen manufacture and urban commercial-financial tools

strengths.zh: 优势在于王权在内战后显著恢复、财政管理趋于稳健、伦敦与呢绒出口带来商业活力、地方乡绅网络能够以较低成本支撑治理，且岛国地理有利于防御
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in the marked recovery of royal authority after civil war, increasingly sound fiscal management, commercial dynamism generated by London and the cloth export trade, a gentry-based local network that supported governance at relatively low cost, and the defensive advantage of island geography

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于常备军规模很小、对呢绒出口和低地市场依赖较深、边境与爱尔兰治理并不稳固，财政虽改善但仍不足以支持长期大规模欧陆战争
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included a very small standing army, deep dependence on cloth exports and Low Countries markets, insecure control of the borders and Ireland, and finances that, though improved, were still insufficient for sustained large-scale continental war

outlook.zh: 1500年的英格兰前景总体乐观。若都铎王朝能维持继承稳定、继续整顿财政并谨慎处理对法对苏关系，英格兰将逐步成长为更集中、更富裕、海上能力更强的王国；但一旦王位继承、宗教统一或对外战争失控，现有稳定仍可能迅速动摇
outlook.en: England's outlook in 1500 was broadly favorable. If the Tudor dynasty maintained stable succession, continued fiscal reform, and handled relations with France and Scotland prudently, England was likely to develop into a more centralized, wealthier, and more maritime-capable kingdom; yet instability in succession, religious unity, or foreign war could still quickly unsettle this stability

description.zh: 都铎王朝初期的英格兰正逐渐加强王权与财政控制。
description.en: Under the early Tudors, England was gradually strengthening royal power and fiscal administration.

governmentForm.zh: 封建君主制，王权在文艺复兴时期逐步加强，并依赖议会、枢密院与地方贵族治理全国
governmentForm.en: Feudal monarchy with strengthening royal authority in the Renaissance period, relying on parliament, privy council, and regional nobility to govern the realm

socialStructure.zh: 社会分为王室与高级贵族、教士、地主骑士与乡绅、自治市市民、佃农与农民，以及高地氏族社会中的族长与附庸成员；低地与高地在制度和文化上存在明显差异
socialStructure.en: Society was divided among the royal house and high nobility, clergy, landed knights and lairds, burgh burgesses, tenants and peasants, as well as clan chiefs and dependents in Highland society; the Lowlands and Highlands differed markedly in institutions and culture

rulingClass.zh: 国王、斯图亚特王朝王室、伯爵与领主、主教与大修道院长，以及在枢密院和议会中服务的低地地主精英
rulingClass.en: The king, the Stewart royal house, earls and lords, bishops and major abbots, and the Lowland landed elite serving in council and parliament

succession.zh: 世袭君主制，通常遵循长子继承原则，但受贵族支持、婚姻联盟与未成年继位风险影响较大
succession.en: Hereditary monarchy, generally following primogeniture, though heavily shaped by noble support, marriage alliances, and the risks of minority successions

structure.zh: 中央政府由国王主持，枢密院处理日常政务，议会批准重要立法与税赋，财政、司法、王室文书与王家领地管理分别由专门官员承担；地方上依靠郡司法长官、王室保安官、教会法庭、自治市与边区监护官维持秩序
structure.en: The central government was headed by the king, with the privy council handling routine administration, parliament approving major legislation and taxation, and specialized officers overseeing finance, justice, royal chancery, and crown lands; locally the kingdom relied on sheriffs, royal justiciars, ecclesiastical courts, burghs, and wardens of the marches

function.zh: 协助国王处理行政、司法、外交、边区治安与重要王令执行
function.en: Assisted the king in administration, justice, diplomacy, border security, and enforcement of major royal commands

function.zh: 管理王室收入、地租、关税、封建义务与账目审计
function.en: Managed royal income, rents, customs, feudal dues, and auditing of accounts

function.zh: 起草和发布特许状、敕令、土地授予与正式文书
function.en: Drafted and issued charters, writs, land grants, and formal instruments

function.zh: 通过司法长官、保安官和中央法庭处理刑事、土地与王权案件
function.en: Handled criminal, land, and crown cases through justiciars, sheriffs, and central courts

function.zh: 管理王家地产、皇家城堡、仓储补给与边防据点
function.en: Administered crown estates, royal castles, stored provisions, and frontier strongholds

function.zh: 负责英苏边境的防务、巡逻、谈判与边民纠纷处理
function.en: Oversaw defense, patrols, negotiation, and dispute settlement along the Anglo-Scottish border

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以郡和保安官辖区为主，低地由地主、郡官与自治市议会治理，高地与群岛则更多依赖氏族领袖、地方领主和半自治传统；王权对偏远地区的控制强弱不一
localAdmin.en: Local administration centered on shires and sheriffdoms: the Lowlands were governed through lairds, county officers, and burgh councils, while the Highlands and Isles relied more on clan chiefs, regional lords, and semi-autonomous custom; royal control varied greatly in remote districts

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合习惯法、封建法、教会法与日益受欧洲大陆影响的罗马法传统；中央法庭、教会法庭与地方保安官法庭并存
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined customary law, feudal law, canon law, and a growing influence from continental Roman law traditions; central courts, church courts, and local sheriff courts coexisted

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖王室领地收入、关税、司法罚金、封建贡赋和议会批准的特别税；日常直接征税能力有限，王权在征收上仍需依赖地方精英合作
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on crown lands, customs duties, judicial fines, feudal dues, and extraordinary taxation approved by parliament; routine direct taxation remained limited, and the monarchy still depended on cooperation from local elites

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，主教区、修道院、圣徒崇拜与教会节日构成公共生活核心，但晚期中世纪已有对教会财政和纪律的批评声音
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the established religion, with dioceses, monasteries, saintly cults, and church festivals forming the core of public life, though late medieval criticism of clerical wealth and discipline was already present

philosophy.zh: 思想上以经院神学、王权合法性、基督教道德秩序与文艺复兴人文主义早期影响并存；王室宫廷与圣安德鲁斯等学术中心开始接触欧洲大陆新学风
philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined scholastic theology, theories of royal legitimacy, Christian moral order, and early Renaissance humanist influence; the royal court and centers such as St Andrews increasingly engaged with new continental learning

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；正式教会和法律文书常用拉丁文，宫廷与低地行政广泛使用中古苏格兰语，盖尔语在高地和群岛主要以口传文化为主，也有少量书写传统
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used throughout; formal ecclesiastical and legal texts often employed Latin, the court and Lowland administration widely used Middle Scots, and Gaelic in the Highlands and Isles remained predominantly oral though not entirely unwritten

culturalAchievements.zh: 詹姆斯四世宫廷以音乐、诗歌、纹章艺术和造船闻名；自治市手工业、教堂建筑、彩绘祭坛、编年史写作与高地盖尔口头诗歌传统都较为活跃，国王本人也支持学术与外科医学发展
culturalAchievements.en: The court of James IV was noted for music, poetry, heraldic culture, and shipbuilding; burgh crafts, church architecture, painted altars, chronicle writing, and Highland Gaelic oral poetry were all active, and the king himself patronized learning and surgery

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言属于日耳曼语族的中古苏格兰语，同时高地和群岛广泛使用凯尔特语族的苏格兰盖尔语；拉丁文是教会与学术共同语
languageFamily.en: The principal language was Middle Scots of the Germanic family, while Scottish Gaelic of the Celtic family was widely spoken in the Highlands and Isles; Latin served as the church and scholarly lingua franca

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧业、羊毛、皮革、渔业和地方手工业为基础，整体规模中等，货币化程度在低地较高，在高地较低。对外贸易集中于羊毛、兽皮、鲑鱼、盐鱼与少量煤炭，依赖法国、低地国家和波罗的海贸易网络
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, pastoralism, wool, hides, fisheries, and local crafts, with a medium overall scale, greater monetization in the Lowlands, and less in the Highlands. Foreign trade focused on wool, hides, salmon, salted fish, and some coal, relying on networks linked to France, the Low Countries, and the Baltic

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、牧羊与养牛、羊毛与皮革加工、渔业、酿酒、盐业、采煤、木材加工、造船与城市手工业
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, sheep and cattle raising, wool and leather processing, fishing, brewing, salt production, coal mining, timber working, shipbuilding, and urban crafts

tradeGoods.zh: 出口羊毛、兽皮、鲑鱼、鳕鱼、盐鱼、煤炭与粗布；进口葡萄酒、奢侈布料、香料、金属制品、书籍、武器和部分谷物
tradeGoods.en: Exports included wool, hides, salmon, cod, salted fish, coal, and coarse cloth; imports included wine, luxury textiles, spices, metalware, books, weapons, and some grain

householdWealth.zh: 大多数家庭财富有限，以土地使用权、牲畜、谷物存量和简单器具为主；低地自治市商人和部分地主家庭积累了较明显的货币与不动产财富，王国贫富差距显著
householdWealth.en: Most households possessed limited wealth, mainly in land-use rights, livestock, grain stocks, and simple tools; Lowland burgh merchants and some landed families accumulated more visible monetary and real-estate wealth, and inequality within the kingdom was marked

economicSystem.zh: 以封建土地关系和自治市商业特权为基础的混合经济，低地市场交换较发达，高地则更依赖氏族、贡赋与实物经济；王室通过港口关税和特许状促进贸易
economicSystem.en: A mixed economy based on feudal land relations and burgh commercial privileges, with more developed market exchange in the Lowlands and greater dependence on clan structures, tribute, and barter in the Highlands; the crown promoted trade through port customs and charters

source.zh: 议会批准税与特别征收

treasuryDescription.zh: 在詹姆斯四世统治下，王室财政较前代稳健，能够维持宫廷、舰船建造和有限军事准备，但财政基础仍然狭窄，遇到长期战争时极易承压
treasuryDescription.en: Under James IV the royal finances were steadier than under earlier reigns, sufficient to sustain the court, shipbuilding, and limited military preparedness, though the fiscal base remained narrow and would come under strain in prolonged war

fiscalPolicy.zh: 政策目标是扩大王室直接收入、强化关税征收、利用司法与特许状获利，并在不激怒贵族与自治市的前提下维持财政稳定
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to enlarge direct royal income, strengthen customs collection, profit from justice and chartering, and preserve stability without provoking the nobility and burghs

description.zh: 王国主力由低地封建征召兵和自治市民兵构成，近年来受欧洲大陆影响，愈发重视长枪密集阵与纪律
description.en: The main strength of the kingdom consisted of Lowland feudal levies and burgh militia, increasingly emphasizing disciplined pike formations under continental influence

description.zh: 由贵族及其扈从组成，数量不多，但在侦察、突击与王室威望展示方面重要
description.en: Composed of nobles and their retainers, not numerous but important for reconnaissance, shock action, and displays of royal prestige

description.zh: 活跃于英苏边境，擅长袭扰、追击和地方防御，具有明显边区战斗传统
description.en: Active along the Anglo-Scottish frontier, they specialized in raiding, pursuit, and local defense and drew on a strong march fighting tradition

description.zh: 王室拥有一定数量火炮与炮手，主要用于攻城和城堡防御，詹姆斯四世对火器和铸炮较为重视
description.en: The crown maintained a modest artillery arm and trained gunners, chiefly for sieges and castle defense, and James IV took notable interest in ordnance and cannon founding

description.zh: 海军规模虽小但在同时代苏格兰较为先进，拥有如“大米迦勒号”一类大型战船的建造能力，用于海上威望、护航与沿岸防务
description.en: Though small, the navy was advanced by Scottish standards of the age, with the capacity to build large warships such as the Great Michael, serving maritime prestige, convoying, and coastal defense

commanderInChief.zh: 詹姆斯四世，苏格兰国王
commanderInChief.en: James IV, King of Scots

technology.zh: 军队处于中世纪晚期向文艺复兴早期过渡阶段，结合长枪方阵、传统冷兵器、少量火绳枪和较成熟的攻城炮；海军造船技术在不列颠北部较突出
technology.en: The military stood in a transition from the late medieval to early Renaissance style, combining pike formations, traditional edged weapons, a small number of handguns, and more developed siege artillery; naval shipbuilding was notable in northern Britain

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自英格兰边境冲突、边区劫掠、岛屿与高地局部不稳，以及一旦履行法苏同盟义务时可能卷入对英战争
threats.en: The main threats came from conflict on the English border, frontier raiding, localized instability in the Isles and Highlands, and the possibility of being drawn into war with England through obligations under the Auld Alliance with France

populationDescription.zh: 1500年前后苏格兰人口大约为50万，主要集中在低地农业区、东海岸城镇和若干教会中心；高地和群岛人口较分散，社会组织更依赖氏族与地方领主
populationDescription.en: Around 1500 Scotland's population was roughly 500,000, concentrated in the Lowland farming districts, east-coast towns, and a few ecclesiastical centers; the Highlands and Isles were more sparsely settled and organized more through clans and local lordship

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王室、大贵族和高级教士；中层为地主、骑士、自治市商人、行会师傅和下级教士；下层为佃农、小农、工匠学徒、雇工与边区或高地附庸人口。高地氏族身份与低地封建身份并行存在
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the royal house, great nobles, and senior clergy; the middle ranks included lairds, knights, burgh merchants, guild masters, and lower clergy; the lower orders were tenants, smallholders, craft apprentices, laborers, and dependent populations in the borderlands or Highlands. Highland clan identity coexisted with Lowland feudal status

allies.zh: 法国是最重要盟友，传统的“法苏同盟”仍是苏格兰对外政策核心；同时与教廷及低地国家保持实用联系
allies.en: France was the principal ally, and the traditional Auld Alliance remained central to Scottish foreign policy; the kingdom also maintained pragmatic ties with the papacy and the Low Countries

enemies.zh: 主要对手是英格兰王国，尽管1500年时并非全面战争状态，但边境紧张、战略互疑和历史敌意长期存在
enemies.en: The principal rival was the Kingdom of England; although not in full-scale war in 1500, border tension, strategic mistrust, and historical hostility persisted

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以维持对英平衡、巩固法苏同盟、提升王室在欧洲宫廷中的地位，并通过婚姻、使节与贸易维护国家安全和王朝利益为主
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on preserving a balance against England, sustaining the Auld Alliance with France, elevating the crown's standing among European courts, and using marriage, diplomacy, and trade to protect the kingdom and dynasty

keyInnovations.zh: 火药武器和攻城炮逐渐普及，王室造船技术发展显著，导航、铸炮、城堡防御改造和大学教育有所进步；不过整体技术水平仍落后于意大利和部分西欧核心地区
keyInnovations.en: Gunpowder weapons and siege artillery were spreading, royal shipbuilding advanced notably, and navigation, cannon founding, castle adaptation, and university learning improved; however, overall technological development still lagged behind Italy and some core regions of western Europe

strengths.zh: 詹姆斯四世统治下政治相对稳定，王权增强，宫廷活跃，海军和炮兵建设有进展；法苏同盟为其提供重要外交支点，低地经济与自治市贸易具备一定活力
strengths.en: Under James IV the kingdom enjoyed relative political stability, stronger monarchy, an active court, and advances in naval and artillery development; the Auld Alliance provided an important diplomatic anchor, while the Lowland economy and burgh trade showed real vitality

weaknesses.zh: 人口与财政规模偏小，国家整合程度有限，高地和群岛控制力不足，对贵族合作依赖很深；若与英格兰爆发长期大战，资源和人力都难以持续支撑
weaknesses.en: Population and fiscal resources were modest, national integration remained incomplete, control over the Highlands and Isles was uneven, and the crown depended heavily on noble cooperation; in a prolonged war with England, resources and manpower would be difficult to sustain

outlook.zh: 1500年的苏格兰前景总体稳定而谨慎乐观，只要避免与英格兰进行破坏性正面战争，王权、贸易和文艺复兴式宫廷文化都有继续发展的空间；但其战略处境依旧脆弱，任何对英冒险都可能逆转局势
outlook.en: In 1500 Scotland's outlook was broadly stable and cautiously optimistic: if it avoided a destructive direct war with England, royal authority, trade, and Renaissance court culture all had room to develop further; yet its strategic position remained fragile, and any reckless confrontation with England could reverse these gains

description.zh: 苏格兰在詹姆斯四世统治下相对稳固，并维持与法国的传统同盟。
description.en: Scotland was relatively secure under James IV and maintained its traditional alliance with France.

name.zh: 西班牙天主教君主联合王权
伊比利亚文明（西班牙王国）

ruler.zh: 斐迪南二世与伊莎贝拉一世

governmentForm.zh: 复合型君主制，由卡斯蒂利亚与阿拉贡在共同王室下联合，保留各自法制、议会与行政传统
governmentForm.en: Composite monarchy uniting Castile and Aragon under a مشترک crown while preserving distinct laws, assemblies, and administrative traditions

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、贵族、教士、城市市民、手工业者、农民与少数改宗群体构成；各地区保留强烈地方身份，卡斯蒂利亚与阿拉贡诸领地差异显著
socialStructure.en: Society was structured around the crown, nobility, clergy, urban burghers, artisans, peasants, and minority convert communities; strong regional identities persisted, with notable differences between the Castilian and Aragonese realms

rulingClass.zh: 王室、高级贵族、大主教与主教、军事修会领袖、王室法学官僚与主要城市寡头
rulingClass.en: The crown, high nobility, archbishops and bishops, leaders of military orders, royal legal bureaucrats, and oligarchies of major towns

succession.zh: 按各王国习惯实行世袭继承；卡斯蒂利亚允许女性继承，王位通过特拉斯塔马拉家族合法传承
succession.en: Hereditary succession according to the customs of each constituent kingdom; Castile permitted female succession, and the throne passed legitimately through the Trastámara line

structure.zh: 中央权力围绕君主及其御前会议运作；卡斯蒂利亚有较强的王室行政体系，阿拉贡诸邦则通过各自议会、法权与总督体系治理。重要机构包括国家会议、财政机构、王室司法机关、宗教裁判所及地方市镇行政网络
structure.en: Central authority operated through the monarchs and their royal councils; Castile possessed a comparatively stronger royal administration, while the Crowns of Aragon were governed through their own cortes, legal privileges, and viceroyal structures. Key institutions included the Council of State, fiscal offices, royal courts, the Inquisition, and municipal administrative networks

function.zh: 处理立法建议、司法申诉、官员任命与王室政策协调，尤其在卡斯蒂利亚发挥核心作用
function.en: Handled legislative advice, judicial appeals, official appointments, and coordination of royal policy, especially in Castile

function.zh: 征收常税与非常税，管理王室收入、租税承包、铸币与债务支付
function.en: Collected ordinary and extraordinary taxes and managed royal income, tax farming, minting, and debt payments

function.zh: 负责高等司法审判、上诉与敕令执行，维护王权法秩序
function.en: Oversaw superior justice, appeals, and enforcement of royal decrees, upholding the legal order of the crown

function.zh: 监督宗教正统、审查改宗者与异端案件，并服务于王室统一政策
function.en: Supervised religious orthodoxy, investigated converts and heresy cases, and served the monarchy’s policies of confessional unity

name.zh: 军事修会与边防管理机构

function.zh: 管理原格拉纳达边地、防御据点、骑士领地与部分军务资源
function.en: Managed the former Granada frontier, defensive strongholds, knightly lordships, and portions of military resources

function.zh: 通过 corregidores 等王室代表监督城市议会、治安、征税与公共秩序
function.en: Used royal representatives such as corregidores to supervise town councils, policing, taxation, and public order

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依托城市议会、王室派驻督政官、领主辖区及教会司法共同运行。卡斯蒂利亚的 corregidor 制度强化王权对城市的控制；阿拉贡、加泰罗尼亚与瓦伦西亚则保留更强的地方特权与法制传统
localAdmin.en: Local administration functioned through town councils, royal governors, lordly jurisdictions, and ecclesiastical courts. In Castile, the corregidor system strengthened royal oversight over towns; Aragon, Catalonia, and Valencia retained stronger local privileges and legal traditions

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系多元并存，包括卡斯蒂利亚法、阿拉贡法、城市特权法、罗马法与教会法。王室法院不断扩大权威，但各领地法权差异依然明显
legalSystem.en: The legal system was plural, combining Castilian law, Aragonese law, municipal fueros, Roman law, and canon law. Royal courts steadily expanded authority, but major legal differences between the realms remained

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以卡斯蒂利亚销售税 alcabala、服务税 servicios、关税、羊毛出口税、教会让渡收入及临时补助为主。阿拉贡诸邦征税须更多依赖议会同意，税制较分散
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied chiefly on the Castilian alcabala sales tax, servicios grants, customs duties, wool export dues, transferred ecclesiastical revenues, and extraordinary levies. In the Aragonese realms taxation depended more heavily on consent from representative assemblies and was more fragmented

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国家核心信仰，王权强调宗教统一；1492年后犹太人被驱逐，穆斯林人口在新征服地区受到持续压力
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the core state faith, and the monarchy emphasized religious unity; after 1492 Jews were expelled, while Muslim populations in newly conquered territories faced increasing pressure

philosophy.zh: 思想上融合经院哲学、晚期中世纪法学、人文主义与王权神授观念；大学和教会仍具主导地位，但意大利式文艺复兴人文主义正逐渐传播
philosophy.en: Intellectual life blended scholasticism, late medieval jurisprudence, humanism, and notions of divinely sanctioned monarchy; universities and the Church remained dominant, while Italian Renaissance humanism spread gradually

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；行政与文学中以卡斯蒂利亚语、加泰罗尼亚语、阿拉贡语及拉丁语为主
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used throughout; Castilian, Catalan, Aragonese, and Latin predominated in administration and literature

culturalAchievements.zh: 完成格拉纳达征服后，王国形成强烈的天主教君权文化；塞维利亚、托莱多与萨拉曼卡成为学术与宗教中心。建筑上延续晚期哥特、穆德哈尔与早期文艺复兴风格；航海知识、地图绘制与编年史写作迅速发展
culturalAchievements.en: After the conquest of Granada, the monarchy fostered a powerful Catholic royal culture; Seville, Toledo, and Salamanca became important centers of learning and religion. Architecture combined late Gothic, Mudéjar, and early Renaissance forms; navigational knowledge, cartography, and chronicle writing advanced rapidly

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属罗曼语族伊比利亚分支，另有巴斯克语等非印欧语言区域存在
languageFamily.en: The principal languages belonged to the Iberian branch of the Romance family, alongside non-Indo-European enclaves such as Basque

gdpDescription.zh: 经济在西欧属强盛水平，卡斯蒂利亚的人口、农业与羊毛出口构成主体，阿拉贡诸地则依赖地中海商业、手工业和区域金融。1492年后格拉纳达并入与大西洋航线开拓增强了增长预期
gdpDescription.en: The economy stood among the stronger ones in Western Europe, with Castile’s population, agriculture, and wool exports forming the core, while the Aragonese lands relied on Mediterranean commerce, crafts, and regional finance. The incorporation of Granada and the opening of Atlantic routes after 1492 improved prospects for growth

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物农业、牧羊业、羊毛生产与出口、葡萄酒、橄榄油、纺织、制革、金属加工、造船、地中海与大西洋贸易
mainIndustries.en: Cereal agriculture, sheep raising, wool production and export, wine, olive oil, textiles, leatherworking, metalworking, shipbuilding, and Mediterranean and Atlantic trade

tradeGoods.zh: 细羊毛、布匹、皮革、铁器、染料、橄榄油、葡萄酒、小麦、糖、奴隶、香料转口品与早期大西洋殖民产物
tradeGoods.en: Fine wool, cloth, leather, iron goods, dyes, olive oil, wine, wheat, sugar, slaves, re-exported spices, and early Atlantic colonial products

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异很大。城市寡头、贵族与教会机构拥有大量地产、税租与羊群；普通农民和手工业者多仅维持中低水平财产，部分地区仍承受地租、什一税与市政摊派压力
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Urban oligarchs, nobles, and ecclesiastical institutions held substantial land, rents, and flocks; ordinary peasants and artisans generally maintained modest property and in many areas faced rents, tithes, and municipal burdens

unit.en: maravedís per year (post-1492 accounting value) annually

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租、城市市场、行会生产、跨区域羊毛贸易和王室特许权相结合的前近代混合经济；国家干预有限但在税收、港口监管、贸易许可和殖民授予方面作用增强
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy combining agrarian rents, urban markets, guild production, interregional wool trade, and royal privileges; state intervention was limited but growing in taxation, port regulation, trade licensing, and colonial grants

source.en: Sales taxes and urban indirect taxation

source.zh: 议会批准补助与特别税
教会让渡收入与十字军税
source.en: Transferred ecclesiastical revenues and crusading taxes

source.zh: 王室领地、司法罚金与特许收入
source.en: Royal domain, judicial fines, and privileged revenues

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库状况相对稳健，但并不宽裕。长期战争、宫廷支出与海外投资要求持续筹资，王室越来越依赖税收预支、信贷与特许收入
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was relatively sound but not abundant. Long wars, court expenses, and overseas ventures required continued fundraising, and the monarchy increasingly depended on tax advances, credit, and privileged revenues

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策倾向于加强王室征收能力、整顿税契、扩大常税基础并利用宗教与战争名义争取额外拨款；同时谨慎维持信用，以支持王朝扩张
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy aimed to strengthen royal collection capacity, regularize tax contracts, broaden ordinary revenue, and secure additional grants under religious and military justifications; at the same time the crown sought to preserve creditworthiness to support dynastic expansion

description.zh: 由长矛兵、剑盾兵、弩手及日益增多的火绳枪手构成，是格拉纳达战争后经验最丰富的主力
description.en: Composed of pikemen, sword-and-buckler men, crossbowmen, and a growing number of arquebusiers; it was the most experienced arm after the Granada campaigns

description.zh: 包括贵族重骑兵、轻骑兵与边地骑士部队，适合侦察、突击与追击
description.en: Included noble heavy cavalry, light cavalry, and frontier-mounted forces suited for reconnaissance, shock action, and pursuit

description.zh: 围城炮兵在15世纪后期显著发展，用于攻城、城防和火器维护
description.en: Siege artillery had expanded significantly in the late fifteenth century and was used in sieges, fortress defense, and firearms maintenance

description.zh: 由王室舰船、征调商船与地中海桨帆战船组成，承担海岸防御、对北非行动与大西洋远航护送
description.en: Consisted of royal vessels, requisitioned merchant shipping, and Mediterranean galleys, serving coastal defense, operations against North Africa, and Atlantic escort duties

description.zh: 由城市、边境地区与领主辖地动员，用于守备、补充与地方治安
description.en: Mobilized from towns, frontier zones, and lordly territories for garrison duty, reinforcement, and local security

commanderInChief.zh: 斐迪南二世为主要统帅，伊莎贝拉一世共同主持战争决策
commanderInChief.en: Ferdinand II served as principal field commander, while Isabella I jointly directed major war policy

technology.zh: 军队正处于中世纪晚期向近代早期过渡阶段，广泛使用火炮、弩与早期火绳枪；攻城战、野战纪律和步骑协同能力在欧洲处于先进水平
technology.en: The military was in transition from late medieval to early modern forms, employing artillery, crossbows, and early arquebuses on a broad scale; siegecraft, battlefield discipline, and infantry-cavalry coordination were advanced by European standards

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自法国在意大利与比利牛斯方向的竞争、北非穆斯林海盗与诸港口势力、奥斯曼海上扩张，以及国内新并入地区的宗教与社会不稳
threats.en: Major threats came from French rivalry in Italy and around the Pyrenees, Muslim corsair and port powers in North Africa, Ottoman maritime expansion, and religious and social instability in newly incorporated territories

populationDescription.zh: 人口总量在欧洲属大型王国，主体集中于卡斯蒂利亚。1492年后格拉纳达并入扩大了统治人口，但犹太人被驱逐、改宗政策与地区差异对人口结构产生明显影响
populationDescription.en: Its total population made it one of the larger kingdoms in Europe, with the majority concentrated in Castile. The annexation of Granada after 1492 enlarged the ruled population, while the expulsion of Jews, conversion policies, and regional differences significantly affected demographic composition

socialClasses.zh: 社会阶层包括王室、世俗贵族、教士、城市寡头、商人、行会匠人、自由农民、佃农、劳役农民、改宗者以及受怀疑和监管的穆德哈尔与摩里斯科群体
socialClasses.en: Social strata included the crown, secular nobility, clergy, urban oligarchs, merchants, guild artisans, free peasants, tenant farmers, laboring peasants, converts, and Mudéjar and Morisco communities subject to suspicion and supervision

allies.zh: 与葡萄牙保持既竞争又协商的关系，并通过婚姻与哈布斯堡王朝建立联系；与教廷保持密切合作，在意大利事务中寻求反法伙伴
allies.en: It maintained a relationship of both rivalry and negotiation with Portugal, forged ties with the Habsburgs through marriage, and cooperated closely with the papacy while seeking anti-French partners in Italian affairs

enemies.zh: 主要对手是法国王国；在地中海与北非方向则面对格拉纳达残余势力之外的穆斯林港口政权、巴巴里海盗网络及更广泛的奥斯曼势力影响
enemies.en: Its principal rival was the Kingdom of France; in the Mediterranean and North African sphere it also faced Muslim port polities, Barbary corsair networks, and the wider influence of Ottoman power

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以王朝联姻、遏制法国、控制伊比利亚、向意大利扩张影响、保障地中海安全并发展大西洋航线与海外据点为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on dynastic marriage, containing France, dominating Iberia, extending influence into Italy, securing the Mediterranean, and developing Atlantic routes and overseas footholds

keyInnovations.zh: 改进型火炮与火绳枪运用、远洋航海技术、地图绘制、天文导航、海船设计、堡垒工程与官僚文书制度均取得显著进步，尤其在远洋航行组织方面迅速追赶并接近葡萄牙水平
keyInnovations.en: Important advances appeared in the use of improved artillery and arquebuses, oceanic navigation, cartography, astronomical piloting, ship design, fortification engineering, and bureaucratic record keeping, especially in the organization of long-distance voyages where Spain was rapidly catching up to Portugal

strengths.zh: 王权集中度提升、宗教动员能力强、人口与资源基础雄厚、军队在围城与火器运用上表现突出，并已开启跨大西洋扩张，使其具备成为欧洲强权的条件
strengths.en: Increasing royal centralization, strong capacity for religious mobilization, a substantial demographic and resource base, and a military notable for siegecraft and firearms gave it the foundations of a major European power, while transatlantic expansion had already begun

weaknesses.zh: 王国仍是法律与制度不完全统一的复合体，财政常受战争拖累；社会对宗教一致性的强制追求损害部分商业与技术群体，地区差异与债务压力长期存在
weaknesses.en: The monarchy remained an incompletely unified composite state with varied legal and institutional systems, and its finances were regularly strained by war; coercive pursuit of religious uniformity harmed some commercial and technical communities, while regional divisions and debt burdens persisted

outlook.zh: 1500年前后的西班牙正处于上升拐点。若能维持财政信用、巩固国内整合并有效管理海外扩张，未来数十年极可能跃升为横跨欧洲与大西洋世界的主导性君主国
outlook.en: Around 1500 Spain stood at a point of ascent. If it could preserve fiscal credit, consolidate domestic integration, and manage overseas expansion effectively, it was highly likely within decades to emerge as a dominant monarchy spanning Europe and the Atlantic world

description.zh: 卡斯蒂利亚与阿拉贡联合后的西班牙正迅速扩张，并开启大西洋帝国建设。
description.en: United under the Catholic Monarchs, Spain was expanding rapidly and beginning its Atlantic imperial project.

description.zh: 米兰是北意大利军事与金融重镇，也是意大利战争核心战场之一。
description.en: Milan was a major military and financial center in northern Italy and one of the principal arenas of the Italian Wars.

rulerTitle.zh: 葡萄牙与阿尔加维国王
rulerTitle.en: King of Portugal and the Algarves

governmentForm.zh: 晚期中世纪至文艺复兴时期的世袭天主教君主制，结合王室议政会、贵族特权与不断扩张的王家官僚体系
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Catholic monarchy of the late medieval and Renaissance period, combining royal council government, noble privilege, and an expanding crown bureaucracy

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室、世俗与教会贵族、城市精英、商人、工匠、农民以及海外据点中的混合社群构成；骑士贵族与教会拥有显著特权，但王权正借助城市、法学家与海贸利益集团加强集中化
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the crown, lay and ecclesiastical nobility, urban elites, merchants, artisans, peasants, and mixed communities in overseas outposts; the knightly nobility and clergy retained major privileges, but royal power was increasingly centralized through towns, jurists, and maritime commercial interests

rulingClass.zh: 国王、王室顾问、高级贵族、主教团、军事修会领袖以及掌控海贸与财政的王家官员
rulingClass.en: The king, royal councillors, high nobility, the episcopate, leaders of military orders, and crown officials controlling maritime trade and finance

succession.zh: 阿维什王朝的世袭君主继承制，以合法男性继承为优先，并由宫廷与贵族承认巩固
succession.en: Hereditary dynastic succession within the House of Aviz, favoring legitimate male inheritance and reinforced by court and noble recognition

structure.zh: 国家以国王为核心，由王室议政会、王室财政机构、司法机关、王家书记局及海外贸易管理机构共同运作。地方上依靠市镇自治机构、王室法官、地方长官和教会网络维持统治。1500年时，随着印度洋航路开辟，里斯本朝廷开始将海外贸易、海军事务与殖民据点管理纳入更直接的王室控制。
structure.en: The state was centered on the king and operated through the royal council, crown financial institutions, judicial bodies, royal chanceries, and overseas trade authorities. In the localities it relied on municipal corporations, royal judges, provincial officers, and church networks. By 1500, with the opening of the Indian Ocean route, the Lisbon court was beginning to bring overseas trade, naval affairs, and colonial strongholds under more direct royal supervision.

function.zh: 负责重大政策、外交、贵族协调、任命与王室顾问事务
function.en: Handled major policy, diplomacy, noble coordination, appointments, and royal advisory affairs

function.zh: 征收王室收入、管理关税、垄断利润、铸币与支出
function.en: Collected crown revenues and managed customs, monopoly income, coinage, and expenditure

function.zh: 起草敕令、保存档案、发布任命状和特许状
function.en: Drafted decrees, preserved archives, and issued appointments and charters

function.zh: 管理王国高等审判、上诉和王室司法监督
function.en: Administered high justice, appeals, and crown judicial oversight across the kingdom

function.zh: 组织远洋舰队、管理香料贸易、监督海外补给站与航海许可
function.en: Organized oceanic fleets, managed the spice trade, and supervised overseas supply stations and navigation licenses

function.zh: 征收港口税、检查货物、监管商船与进口商品
function.en: Collected port duties, inspected cargoes, and regulated merchant shipping and imported goods

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政主要通过市镇议会、科雷热多尔等王室司法官、贵族领地官员和教区组织实施。王权在核心地区较强，在偏远地区与岛屿则需与地方贵族、修会和市镇自治传统妥协。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted mainly through municipal councils, royal judicial officers such as corregedores, seigneurial officers on noble lands, and parish organization. Royal power was stronger in the core regions, while in remote districts and islands it operated through compromise with local nobles, religious orders, and municipal traditions.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以罗马法、教会法、王室敕令和习惯法相结合，15世纪末形成较系统的王室法典化趋势。司法既体现等级差异，也反映王权试图削弱封建割据、加强统一审判权的努力。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman law, canon law, royal ordinances, and customary law, with a growing trend toward more systematic royal codification by the late 15th century. Justice reflected social hierarchy but also the crown’s effort to reduce feudal fragmentation and strengthen unified judicial authority.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收来源包括土地与封建性收入、王室专卖、港口关税、海外贸易利润、消费税及经王国议会或特许程序批准的特别征收。对外贸易，尤其是黄金、香料、奴隶与进口奢侈品的税收日益重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation drew on land and feudal revenues, royal monopolies, port customs, overseas trade profits, consumption taxes, and extraordinary levies approved through the realm’s political mechanisms. Taxes on foreign trade—especially gold, spices, slaves, and imported luxuries—were becoming increasingly important.

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为国教，王权与教会关系紧密，宗教节庆、修道院、主教区和军事修会在社会生活中占据核心地位。收复失地运动的记忆与对外扩张的宗教合法性相互结合。
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the state religion, and the crown maintained a close relationship with the Church. Religious festivals, monasteries, dioceses, and military orders stood at the center of social life. The memory of the Reconquista combined with the religious legitimation of overseas expansion.

philosophy.zh: 思想上以经院哲学、基督教王权观与文艺复兴人文主义初步输入并存；实用航海知识、宇宙地理观和王室服务伦理逐渐形成独特的海洋帝国意识。
philosophy.en: Intellectually, scholasticism, Christian theories of kingship, and the early influence of Renaissance humanism coexisted; practical navigational knowledge, cosmographical thinking, and an ethic of royal service gradually formed a distinctive maritime imperial outlook.

writingSystem.zh: 使用拉丁字母书写葡萄牙语，拉丁语仍广泛用于教会、学术、法律和正式文书
writingSystem.en: Used the Latin alphabet to write Portuguese, while Latin remained widely employed in church, scholarship, law, and formal documentation

culturalAchievements.zh: 葡萄牙在1500年前后最突出的文化成就是航海图绘制、远洋航海技术、宫廷宗教艺术以及后来被称为曼努埃尔风格的建筑萌芽。里斯本成为汇聚欧洲、非洲和亚洲商品与知识的港口文化中心。
culturalAchievements.en: Portugal’s most notable cultural achievements around 1500 were cartography, oceanic navigation techniques, courtly religious art, and the early emergence of what would later be called Manueline architecture. Lisbon was becoming a port-cultural center where goods and knowledge from Europe, Africa, and Asia converged.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系罗曼语族伊比利亚-西部支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Romance branch, Western Iberian group

gdpDescription.zh: 1500年的葡萄牙是欧洲规模不大的王国，但以人均财富、海关收入和海外商业潜力而言极具活力。农业仍是基础，然而几内亚黄金、西非奴隶贸易、大西洋糖业以及通往印度的新航路使其经济远超单纯本土农业国家的水平。
gdpDescription.en: Portugal in 1500 was a relatively small European kingdom, but it was exceptionally dynamic in per-capita wealth, customs revenue, and overseas commercial potential. Agriculture remained the base, yet Guinea gold, the West African slave trade, Atlantic sugar production, and the new route to India raised its economy well above that of a purely land-based agrarian state.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物与葡萄种植、畜牧业、渔业、食盐生产、造船、航海服务、纺织与皮革加工、金属工艺、港口贸易、几内亚黄金转运、马德拉和大西洋群岛糖业
mainIndustries.en: Grain and vine cultivation, livestock, fishing, salt production, shipbuilding, navigation services, textiles and leatherworking, metal crafts, port commerce, Guinea gold transit, and sugar production in Madeira and the Atlantic islands

tradeGoods.zh: 出口与转口商品包括盐、葡萄酒、橄榄油、鱼类、糖、黄金、象牙、奴隶、珊瑚、染料、纺织品以及开始出现的香料；进口商品包括小麦、高级布匹、金属制品、奢侈消费品以及来自东方的胡椒、肉桂和宝石
tradeGoods.en: Exported and re-exported goods included salt, wine, olive oil, fish, sugar, gold, ivory, slaves, coral, dyestuffs, textiles, and increasingly spices; imports included wheat, fine cloth, metal goods, luxury wares, and eastern products such as pepper, cinnamon, and gems

householdWealth.zh: 普通农户财富有限，以土地使用权、牲畜、工具和粮食储备为主；沿海商人、船主和王室承包商则可能积累远高于欧洲平均水平的流动财富。海外贸易带来的财富分配极不均衡，但推动了里斯本和主要港口的繁荣。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary peasant households possessed limited wealth, mainly in the form of land-use rights, livestock, tools, and grain stores; coastal merchants, shipowners, and crown contractors could accumulate liquid wealth well above the European average. Overseas commerce produced highly unequal distribution of wealth but drove prosperity in Lisbon and the main ports.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体系为以农业为基础、以王室特许和海上商业垄断为特色的前近代混合经济。私人商人、外国商帮、市镇市场和王室垄断并存，其中国王对黄金、胡椒、关键海路和殖民据点收入的干预迅速加强。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was a premodern mixed economy based on agriculture but distinguished by crown charters and maritime commercial monopolies. Private merchants, foreign trading communities, municipal markets, and royal monopolies coexisted, while the king’s intervention over gold, pepper, strategic sea routes, and colonial stronghold revenues was rapidly increasing.

source.zh: 海外贸易垄断与王室商利
source.en: Overseas trade monopolies and crown commercial profit

source.zh: 土地、封建与司法性收入

source.en: Consumption taxes and domestic indirect levies

source.zh: 教会贡献、特许金与其他收入
source.en: Clerical contributions, charter fees, and other income

category.zh: 宫廷、王室恩赏与贵族俸给
category.en: Court, royal patronage, and noble stipends

treasuryDescription.zh: 王室金库因几内亚黄金流入和新近的印度航路收益预期而较为充实，但支出压力同样巨大。舰队建造、远洋补给、宫廷炫耀和海外驻防使财政对贸易成功高度敏感。
treasuryDescription.en: The royal treasury was comparatively well supplied thanks to the inflow of Guinea gold and expectations from the new India route, but expenditure pressures were equally great. Fleet construction, long-distance provisioning, court display, and overseas garrisons made the fiscal system highly sensitive to commercial success.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策倾向于扩大海关和垄断收入，以海外贸易利润支持宫廷、海军和宗教建筑。王权宁可强化港口征管和商业专控，而非过度压榨本土农业税基，从而维持国内稳定并促进航海投资。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored expanding customs and monopoly income, using overseas commercial profits to support the court, navy, and religious construction. The crown preferred tighter port enforcement and commercial control rather than overburdening the domestic agrarian tax base, thereby preserving internal stability and encouraging maritime investment.

description.zh: 由王室直属部队、贵族骑士、步兵和边境守军组成，主要任务是国土防御、城堡驻守及对北非据点的支援
description.en: Comprised crown troops, noble cavalry, infantry, and frontier garrisons, tasked mainly with homeland defense, castle garrisons, and support for North African positions

description.zh: 包括武装卡拉维尔、卡拉克大帆船、护航船员、炮手和海军士兵，是王国最具战略价值的力量，承担印度航路、非洲海岸和大西洋岛屿的护航与作战
description.en: Included armed caravels, carracks, escort crews, gunners, and naval soldiers, and was the kingdom’s most strategically valuable force, responsible for convoying and fighting along the India route, the African coast, and the Atlantic islands

description.zh: 由市镇和地方社区按需征集，用于紧急防御、港口警备和后方守备
description.en: Raised by municipalities and local communities as needed for emergency defense, port security, and rear-area protection

commanderInChief.zh: 曼努埃尔一世国王
commanderInChief.en: King Manuel I

technology.zh: 葡萄牙军事实力的核心在于航海与海战技术，包括远洋卡拉维尔、重型卡拉克、改良罗盘与天文导航、海图学以及舰载火炮运用。陆战方面仍以中世纪后期城堡、防御工事、长柄武器、弩、火绳火器和野战炮兵的混合体系为主。
technology.en: The core of Portuguese military strength lay in naval and maritime technology, including ocean-going caravels, heavy carracks, improved compass and celestial navigation, cartography, and the use of shipboard artillery. On land, warfare still relied on a late medieval mix of castles, fortifications, polearms, crossbows, matchlock firearms, and field artillery.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北非穆斯林沿岸势力、海上私掠、与卡斯蒂利亚潜在的伊比利亚竞争，以及在印度洋与阿拉伯、古吉拉特和其他穆斯林商贸网络的冲突。远洋航线漫长也使补给、疾病和船难成为持续威胁。
threats.en: Main threats came from Muslim powers on the North African littoral, maritime privateering, potential Iberian rivalry with Castile, and conflict in the Indian Ocean with Arab, Gujarati, and other Muslim commercial networks. The great length of oceanic routes also made logistics, disease, and shipwreck constant dangers.

populationDescription.zh: 葡萄牙本土人口约120万，规模在欧洲属中小王国，但人口密度在沿海与河谷地带较高。社会以农村为主，不过里斯本、波尔图和大西洋港口正因海外贸易而显著扩张。
populationDescription.en: Mainland Portugal had a population of about 1.2 million, making it a small-to-medium European kingdom, though population density was higher in coastal and riverine districts. Society remained predominantly rural, but Lisbon, Porto, and Atlantic ports were expanding notably because of overseas trade.

socialClasses.zh: 主要阶层包括王室与高等贵族、低级骑士与地方绅士、神职人员、城市商人和手工业者、海员与港口劳工、自由农民、佃农及少量奴隶人口。里斯本和南部港口存在非洲裔、犹太裔改宗者以及外国商人社群，使社会呈现比多数欧洲王国更强的海港多样性。
socialClasses.en: Major social strata included the crown and high nobility, lesser knights and local gentry, clergy, urban merchants and artisans, sailors and dock laborers, free peasants, tenants, and a small enslaved population. Lisbon and the southern ports contained African-descended people, Jewish converts, and foreign merchant communities, giving society a stronger port-city diversity than most European kingdoms.

allies.zh: 与英格兰保持长期同盟，与教廷关系密切，并通过与卡斯蒂利亚-阿拉贡王室的婚姻和条约维持复杂但总体可控的伊比利亚关系
allies.en: Maintained a long-standing alliance with England, close relations with the papacy, and a complex but generally manageable Iberian relationship with the crowns of Castile and Aragon through marriage and treaty

enemies.zh: 在北非和印度洋主要面对穆斯林港口势力、马穆鲁克与奥斯曼影响网络，以及海上竞争者与私掠者；在条约边界之外，也与其他欧洲势力存在潜在殖民竞争
enemies.en: In North Africa and the Indian Ocean it mainly faced Muslim port powers, Mamluk and Ottoman influence networks, as well as maritime rivals and privateers; beyond treaty boundaries it also faced potential colonial rivalry from other European powers

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以海洋扩张、教廷授权、商路控制和据点体系建设为核心。葡萄牙致力于绕过地中海中间商，直接连接西非黄金、印度香料和远东贸易，并通过条约、远征和堡垒港口建立海上帝国雏形。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on maritime expansion, papal authorization, trade-route control, and the construction of a network of fortified bases. Portugal sought to bypass Mediterranean middlemen, connect directly with West African gold, Indian spices, and Far Eastern trade, and build the first outlines of a seaborne empire through treaties, expeditions, and fortress ports.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键创新包括远洋卡拉维尔与卡拉克设计、阿斯特罗拉布和天文导航的实际应用、系统海图绘制、季风与洋流知识积累、舰载火炮整合、港口后勤组织以及跨洋航线经验。葡萄牙在1500年并非欧洲最全面的技术强国，但在远洋航海这一关键领域居于世界前列。
keyInnovations.en: Key innovations included ocean-going caravel and carrack design, practical use of the astrolabe and celestial navigation, systematic chartmaking, accumulated knowledge of monsoons and currents, integration of shipboard artillery, port logistics organization, and transoceanic routing experience. In 1500 Portugal was not Europe’s most broadly advanced technological power, but in long-distance navigation it stood among the world leaders.

strengths.zh: 葡萄牙的优势在于王权相对集中、海军和航海技术领先、里斯本港口地位突出、海外贸易利润丰厚、与英格兰及教廷关系稳固，以及国家战略目标高度明确。其规模虽小，却能通过海上力量放大影响力。
strengths.en: Portugal’s strengths lay in relatively concentrated royal authority, leading naval and navigational capabilities, the prominence of Lisbon as a port, rich overseas trading profits, durable ties with England and the papacy, and unusually clear strategic objectives. Though small in size, it could magnify its influence through sea power.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于人口与资源基础有限，对海贸收益依赖过强，远洋补给线脆弱，海外据点分散，国内农业生产并不特别强大，且贵族恩赏、宗教政治压力与大规模扩张成本可能迅速侵蚀财政余裕。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were a limited population and resource base, overdependence on maritime profits, vulnerable long-distance supply lines, dispersed overseas outposts, a domestic agrarian base that was not exceptionally strong, and the risk that noble patronage, religious-political pressures, and the costs of large-scale expansion could quickly erode fiscal reserves.

outlook.zh: 1500年的葡萄牙前景极为光明，正处于由欧洲边缘王国转变为跨洋商业帝国的关键时刻。如果能够维持海军优势、稳固印度航路并控制香料贸易，其财富与国际地位将继续上升；但若扩张过快、遭遇强劲海上对手或财政管理失衡，其小国结构性限制也会迅速显现。
outlook.en: Portugal’s outlook in 1500 was exceptionally bright, as it stood at the turning point from a peripheral European kingdom to a transoceanic commercial empire. If it could maintain naval superiority, secure the India route, and control the spice trade, its wealth and international standing would continue to rise; but if expansion outran its means, if it encountered strong maritime opponents, or if fiscal management faltered, the structural limits of a small kingdom would quickly become visible.

description.zh: 葡萄牙正处于海外扩张高峰，控制通往印度洋的海上航线。
description.en: Portugal was at the height of its overseas expansion and was establishing control over routes into the Indian Ocean.

name.zh: 意大利海洋文明（威尼斯共和国）

ruler.zh: 阿戈斯蒂诺·巴尔巴里戈

dynasty.zh: 无固定王朝，由威尼斯贵族家族轮选
dynasty.en: No fixed dynasty; elective among Venetian patrician families

governmentForm.zh: 寡头共和制，由贵族议会、元老院与总督共同治理
governmentForm.en: Oligarchic republic governed jointly by patrician councils, the Senate, and the Doge

socialStructure.zh: 社会以封闭的贵族阶层为统治核心，其下为富裕市民、商人、行会工匠、船员、教士、乡村农民与属地居民。海上帝国中的达尔马提亚、希腊与本土意大利臣属在法律与政治权利上低于威尼斯本城贵族。
socialStructure.en: Society was centered on a closed patrician class as the ruling core, beneath which stood wealthy cittadini, merchants, guild artisans, sailors, clergy, rural peasants, and subject populations. In the maritime empire, Dalmatian, Greek, and mainland Italian subjects held fewer political rights than the patricians of Venice proper.

rulingClass.zh: 登记在《金书》中的威尼斯贵族家族
rulingClass.en: Venetian patrician families inscribed in the Golden Book

succession.zh: 总督由贵族通过复杂的多轮抽签与投票程序终身选举产生，并非世袭
succession.en: The Doge was elected for life by the patriciate through a complex multi-stage process of lot and voting, not by hereditary succession

structure.zh: 国家政体由大议会作为贵族共同体基础，元老院负责外交、财政与战略，十人委员会负责国家安全，总督为礼仪性与协调性首脑，另有总督顾问、四十人法院及驻外官员构成严密官僚网络。
structure.en: The state was structured around the Great Council as the base of the patrician body, the Senate for diplomacy, finance, and strategy, the Council of Ten for state security, the Doge as ceremonial and coordinating head, and a dense bureaucratic network including ducal councillors, the Quarantia courts, and overseas officials.

function.zh: 构成共和国主权基础，确认贵族成员资格并选举主要官职
function.en: Formed the sovereign base of the republic, confirming patrician membership and electing major offices

function.zh: 处理外交、商业、海军调度、财政与对外战争事务
function.en: Handled diplomacy, commerce, naval deployment, finance, and external war policy

function.zh: 负责国家安全、反叛侦缉、机密事务与非常时期权力运作
function.en: Oversaw state security, anti-conspiracy investigations, secret matters, and emergency powers

function.zh: 管理高级司法、财政审理与若干选举程序
function.en: Managed high justice, fiscal adjudication, and parts of the electoral process

function.zh: 监督威尼斯兵工厂、舰船建造、海员征募与军需供应
function.en: Supervised the Arsenal, shipbuilding, mariner recruitment, and naval supply

name.zh: 本土与海外属地行政署

function.zh: 管理威尼斯在意大利本土及达尔马提亚、希腊诸殖民地的总督、税收与司法
function.en: Administered Venetian governors, taxation, and justice in the mainland dominions and colonies in Dalmatia and Greece

function.zh: 征收关税、盐税、铸币收入、公债管理与国家账目
function.en: Collected customs, salt taxes, mint revenues, public debt management, and state accounts

localAdmin.zh: 威尼斯本城由各区与诸委员会分工治理；本土领地由总督、长官、驻城官与法官管理；海外属地则由公派总督、海港长官与税务官监督。地方自治有限，但商业城市常保有若干旧法与习惯。
localAdmin.en: Venice itself was governed through districts and numerous committees; mainland territories were administered by rectors, podestàs, captains, and judges; overseas possessions were supervised by appointed governors, port officials, and fiscal officers. Local autonomy was limited, though commercial cities often retained parts of their older laws and customs.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合罗马法传统、威尼斯习惯法、商法与国家敕令。贵族受政治法约束，商人合同与海事案件尤其发达，国家安全案件常由十人委员会以较秘密和强力方式处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman-law traditions, Venetian customary law, commercial law, and state decrees. Patricians were regulated by political statutes, merchant contracts and maritime cases were highly developed, and affairs touching state security were often handled by the Council of Ten with greater secrecy and force.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依靠关税、港口税、盐专卖、铸币收益、属地贡赋、财产性征收与战争时期特别贷款。相比直接土地税，威尼斯更倚赖贸易流通和国家垄断收入。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue relied chiefly on customs duties, port taxes, the salt monopoly, mint profits, territorial tribute, property-based levies, and wartime forced loans. Compared with direct land taxes, Venice depended more heavily on commercial circulation and state monopolies.

religion.zh: 拉丁基督教（罗马天主教）为国教，但威尼斯传统上强调国家对教会事务的自主性，并在商业往来中容纳东正教徒、犹太人和穆斯林商人。
religion.en: Latin Christianity (Roman Catholicism) was the state religion, but Venice traditionally stressed state autonomy in ecclesiastical affairs and accommodated Orthodox Christians, Jews, and Muslim merchants in commercial dealings.

philosophy.zh: 政治思想受公民共和传统、基督教道德、人文主义与务实商业理性影响。威尼斯人重视国家稳定、制度平衡、荣誉、节制与公共利益。
philosophy.en: Political thought was shaped by civic republican tradition, Christian morality, humanism, and pragmatic commercial rationality. Venetians valued state stability, institutional balance, honor, restraint, and the common good.

writingSystem.zh: 以拉丁字母书写拉丁文和威尼斯语，官方文书多用拉丁文与意大利化行政文体，商业账册亦广泛使用地方俗语。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used for Latin and Venetian; official records often employed Latin and Italianate administrative prose, while business ledgers widely used the local vernacular.

culturalAchievements.zh: 威尼斯在文艺复兴时期以绘画、建筑、印刷出版、玻璃工艺、制图学与音乐著称。圣马可广场及总督宫象征国家荣光，穆拉诺玻璃、精细书籍装帧和海图制作在欧洲享有盛名。
culturalAchievements.en: During the Renaissance, Venice was renowned for painting, architecture, printing, glassmaking, cartography, and music. St Mark's Square and the Doge's Palace symbolized state grandeur, while Murano glass, fine book production, and chartmaking were celebrated across Europe.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系—罗曼语族；官方与学术用拉丁文，日常与商业多用威尼斯语及其他意大利方言
languageFamily.en: Indo-European, Romance branch; Latin for official and learned use, Venetian and other Italian vernaculars for daily and commercial life

gdpDescription.zh: 1500年前后的威尼斯共和国属于欧洲最富裕、商业化程度最高的国家之一。其财富并不主要来自广阔农业腹地，而是依靠地中海中转贸易、造船业、金融、公债市场、工艺制造以及对亚得里亚海航线和东地中海港口网络的控制。
gdpDescription.en: Around 1500, the Republic of Venice was among the wealthiest and most commercialized states in Europe. Its wealth derived less from a broad agrarian hinterland than from Mediterranean transit trade, shipbuilding, finance, public debt markets, manufacturing, and control of Adriatic routes and eastern Mediterranean port networks.

mainIndustries.zh: 远洋贸易、造船、军械制造、玻璃、纺织、丝绸加工、印刷出版、金属加工、盐业、金融与保险
mainIndustries.en: Long-distance trade, shipbuilding, armaments production, glassmaking, textiles, silk processing, printing, metalworking, salt, finance, and insurance

tradeGoods.zh: 香料、胡椒、丝绸、棉布、糖、盐、谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、木材、金属制品、奴隶、玻璃器皿与奢侈工艺品
tradeGoods.en: Spices, pepper, silk, cotton textiles, sugar, salt, grain, wine, olive oil, timber, metal goods, slaves, glassware, and luxury crafts

householdWealth.zh: 贵族与大商人家庭财富极高，常持有船队、仓库、债券和地产；城市中产由商人、书记员和工匠构成；普通船员和工匠收入波动明显；乡村农户与属地居民生活水平低于威尼斯城内居民。
householdWealth.en: Patrician and major merchant households were extremely wealthy, often holding fleets, warehouses, bonds, and land; the urban middle strata consisted of merchants, clerks, and artisans; ordinary sailors and craftsmen faced volatile earnings; rural households and subject populations lived at lower levels than residents of Venice itself.

economicSystem.zh: 国家保护下的商业资本主义与海洋帝国经济。共和国通过舰队护航、专卖制度、特许经营、公共债务和外交协定维持市场秩序，同时允许私人商人广泛逐利。
economicSystem.en: A state-protected commercial capitalism embedded in a maritime imperial economy. The republic maintained market order through convoy protection, monopolies, chartered privileges, public debt, and diplomatic agreements, while allowing private merchants broad room for profit-seeking.

source.en: Taxes and tribute from mainland and overseas territories

source.zh: 铸币、司法罚金与行政收费
source.en: Mint profits, judicial fines, and administrative fees
Public debt operations and extraordinary levies

category.zh: 陆军与意大利战争支出
公共工程、粮食与应急储备
category.en: Public works, grain supply, and reserves

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库以现金储备、金银铸币、盐业收益预期与国家公债信用共同构成。威尼斯拥有欧洲最成熟的公共财政信用体系之一，虽因战争支出压力巨大，但整体偿债能力仍强。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury consisted of cash reserves, gold and silver coin, anticipated salt revenues, and the credit of the public debt. Venice possessed one of Europe's most developed systems of public fiscal credit; despite heavy wartime burdens, its overall debt-serving capacity remained strong.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向谨慎与信用维护：平时依靠关税和专卖收入，战时扩大借贷、临时税与属地征发，并尽力避免破坏商人对共和国偿付能力的信心。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy favored caution and credit preservation: in peacetime relying on customs and monopoly income, in wartime expanding borrowing, extraordinary taxation, and territorial requisitions, while striving to avoid undermining merchant confidence in the republic's solvency.

description.zh: 威尼斯的核心武力，由桨帆战船、武装商船、水手、海军步兵和兵工厂技术人员构成，负责护航、封锁和亚得里亚海制海权。
description.en: The core arm of Venetian power, composed of galleys, armed merchantmen, sailors, naval infantry, and Arsenal technicians, responsible for convoy protection, blockade, and control of the Adriatic.

description.zh: 主要部署在威尼斯本土领地，包含步兵、骑兵、炮兵及要塞守军，常与意大利雇佣兵传统结合，并在大战时吸纳外国专业士兵。
description.en: Mainly deployed on the Venetian mainland, including infantry, cavalry, artillery, and garrisons, often tied to the Italian condottieri tradition and supplemented in major wars by foreign professional soldiers.

description.zh: 由达尔马提亚、希腊据点、岛屿守备队、港口民兵和战时征召人员组成，用于守城、海岸防御与补充作战。
description.en: Composed of Dalmatian and Greek garrisons, island troops, port militias, and wartime levies used for city defense, coastal security, and reinforcement.

commanderInChief.zh: 总督名义统帅；实际由海军总司令、本土总司令与元老院统筹
commanderInChief.en: The Doge was nominal commander-in-chief; actual direction was shared by the Captain General of the Sea, mainland commanders, and the Senate

technology.zh: 军事技术处于文艺复兴欧洲前列，擅长桨帆舰建造、港口防御、铸炮、军械生产和海上后勤。兵工厂具备准流水式组织能力，能较快装备舰队，但陆军战术正在面对法国重骑兵与火器步兵新趋势的挑战。
technology.en: Military technology was among the most advanced in Renaissance Europe, especially in galley construction, port defense, cannon founding, arms production, and naval logistics. The Arsenal had proto-industrial organizational capacity for rapid fleet outfitting, though army tactics were increasingly challenged by new trends in French heavy cavalry and firearm infantry.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自奥斯曼帝国在东地中海的扩张、意大利战争中法国与神圣罗马帝国势力的介入，以及海上贸易路线被葡萄牙绕过好望角后带来的长期竞争压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from Ottoman expansion in the eastern Mediterranean, intervention by France and the Holy Roman Empire in the Italian Wars, and the long-term competitive pressure created by Portuguese sea routes around the Cape of Good Hope.

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括威尼斯本城、本土领地诸城镇、乡村居民，以及达尔马提亚和希腊诸海上属地居民。首都威尼斯本身是欧洲最大城市之一，但共和国整体人口分布在多个港口、商镇和农业腹地之中。
populationDescription.en: The population included Venice proper, the towns and countryside of the mainland dominions, and the maritime subjects of Dalmatia and Greece. Venice itself was one of Europe's largest cities, but the republic's total population was distributed across many ports, market towns, and agricultural hinterlands.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为世袭贵族；其次为有资格担任文职的市民阶层；再下为商人、船主、行会工匠、店主与书记员；大量水手、码头工、女佣与临时工构成城市劳动群体；乡村则以佃农、自耕农和雇农为主；海外属地另有希腊东正教居民、达尔马提亚人、犹太社群与外来商人。
socialClasses.en: At the top stood the hereditary patriciate; beneath them the cittadini eligible for civil service; below them merchants, shipowners, guild artisans, shopkeepers, and clerks; large numbers of sailors, dockworkers, servants, and casual laborers formed the urban workforce; the countryside was dominated by tenants, smallholders, and laborers; overseas territories also included Greek Orthodox populations, Dalmatians, Jewish communities, and foreign merchants.

allies.zh: 1500年时威尼斯在意大利战争中与法国王国结盟，共同反对米兰公国；同时维持与教廷、哈布斯堡、奥斯曼及诸意大利国家间灵活而多变的关系。
allies.en: In 1500 Venice was allied with the Kingdom of France in the Italian Wars against the Duchy of Milan, while maintaining flexible and shifting relations with the Papacy, the Habsburgs, the Ottomans, and other Italian states.

enemies.zh: 直接敌对者主要是米兰公国及其支持者；长期战略对手则包括奥斯曼帝国，以及在海上香料贸易中日益形成竞争的葡萄牙。
enemies.en: Its immediate enemies were chiefly the Duchy of Milan and its supporters; long-term strategic rivals included the Ottoman Empire and, in the spice trade, an increasingly competitive Portugal.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护贸易通道、保持亚得里亚海霸权、防止单一强权控制意大利，并通过均势外交保护共和国独立为核心。威尼斯偏好务实结盟、商业条约和有限战争，而非意识形态性扩张。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving trade routes, maintaining hegemony in the Adriatic, preventing any single power from dominating Italy, and protecting republican independence through balance-of-power diplomacy. Venice preferred pragmatic alliances, commercial treaties, and limited war over ideological expansion.

keyInnovations.zh: 威尼斯的关键优势在于兵工厂的大规模造船组织、先进海图与导航知识、铸炮技术、玻璃制造、印刷出版与金融文书体系。其行政档案管理、复式簿记运用和海上保险实践也十分成熟。
keyInnovations.en: Venice's key strengths lay in the large-scale shipbuilding organization of the Arsenal, advanced charting and navigational knowledge, cannon founding, glassmaking, printing, and financial documentation. Its archival administration, use of double-entry bookkeeping, and maritime insurance practices were also highly developed.

strengths.zh: 威尼斯拥有强大商贸网络、成熟财政信用、先进海军工业、稳定寡头制度和出色外交传统。其资本、信息、港口与舰队的结合，使其在1500年仍是地中海最重要的商业强国之一。
strengths.en: Venice possessed a powerful trade network, sophisticated fiscal credit, advanced naval industry, a stable oligarchic constitution, and a strong diplomatic tradition. The combination of capital, information, ports, and fleets made it one of the Mediterranean's foremost commercial powers in 1500.

weaknesses.zh: 国家人口与领土规模有限，陆军资源不如大型君主国；贵族政治排他，社会流动受限；经济高度依赖中转贸易，易受奥斯曼战争、航路变迁和欧洲大国竞争冲击。
weaknesses.en: The state had limited demographic and territorial depth compared with large monarchies, and its land forces were weaker than those of major kingdoms; patrician politics were exclusionary and constrained social mobility; the economy depended heavily on transit trade and was vulnerable to Ottoman war, route changes, and competition from great powers.

outlook.zh: 短期内威尼斯仍将保持富裕、稳定与外交影响力，并在意大利和东地中海继续扮演关键角色；但从长期看，奥斯曼压力、意大利战争加剧以及大西洋贸易兴起将逐步侵蚀其传统优势。
outlook.en: In the short term Venice would remain wealthy, stable, and diplomatically influential, continuing to play a key role in Italy and the eastern Mediterranean; in the longer run, however, Ottoman pressure, intensifying Italian Wars, and the rise of Atlantic trade would gradually erode its traditional advantages.

description.zh: 威尼斯是地中海贸易强国，也是意大利战争中的关键参与者。
description.en: Venice was a major Mediterranean trading power and a central participant in the Italian Wars.

name.zh: 意大利文艺复兴文明（佛罗伦萨共和国）

governmentForm.zh: 寡头主导的城市共和国，保留公民共和制度与行会政治传统
governmentForm.en: An oligarchically guided city-republic that retained civic republican institutions and guild-based political traditions

socialStructure.zh: 社会以城市精英、行会成员、工匠、金融家、乡村佃农与雇工构成；政治权力集中于富裕家族与主要行会，但共和国名义上由公民机构共同治理
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of urban elites, guild members, artisans, financiers, rural sharecroppers, and wage laborers; political power was concentrated in wealthy families and major guilds, though the republic was formally governed through civic institutions

rulingClass.zh: 富裕商人银行家家族、主要行会领袖、受过人文教育的城市官员与少数土地贵族
rulingClass.en: Wealthy merchant-banking families, leaders of the major guilds, humanist-trained civic officials, and a small stratum of landed nobles

succession.zh: 国家元首不按世袭继承，而由共和国宪制安排与政治协商产生；终身正义旗手由主要政治集团支持下选出
succession.en: The head of state did not succeed hereditarily but emerged through republican constitutional procedures and political negotiation; the Gonfaloniere for Life was chosen with support from the leading political factions

structure.zh: 佛罗伦萨政体由大议会、执政团、八人委员会、财政与司法机关共同构成。终身正义旗手为国家象征与行政协调者，但实际施政依赖各委员会、书记官系统与 influential 家族网络。
structure.en: The Florentine polity was composed of the Great Council, executive magistracies, the Eight, financial offices, and judicial institutions. The Gonfaloniere for Life served as the symbolic head and administrative coordinator, but actual governance depended on committees, chanceries, and influential family networks.

function.zh: 负责共和国日常决策、政令发布、危机应对与议会协调
function.en: Handled daily republican decision-making, issuance of decrees, crisis response, and coordination with councils

function.zh: 管理防务、雇佣兵合同、边境驻防与战时财政安排
function.en: Managed defense, mercenary contracts, frontier garrisons, and wartime financial arrangements

function.zh: 征收直接税与间接税，管理公债、国库、盐税与关税
function.en: Collected direct and indirect taxes and administered public debt, the treasury, salt duties, and customs

function.zh: 监督法院、刑罚执行、商业纠纷审理及城市治安
function.en: Oversaw courts, criminal enforcement, commercial disputes, and urban order

function.zh: 起草公文、维持使节往来、收集情报并管理条约记录
function.en: Drafted state papers, maintained embassies, gathered intelligence, and kept treaty records

function.zh: 协调行会事务、监督度量衡、市场秩序与制造业标准
function.en: Coordinated guild affairs and supervised weights, measures, market order, and manufacturing standards

localAdmin.zh: 共和国核心由佛罗伦萨城区与周边托斯卡纳属地组成，地方由城市任命的长官、乡镇法官、税务员与驻军官管理；比萨、阿雷佐、普拉托等地保留部分地方惯例，但受佛罗伦萨财政与司法控制。
localAdmin.en: The republic centered on Florence and its Tuscan subject territories, administered by city-appointed governors, rural judges, tax collectors, and military officials; places such as Pisa, Arezzo, and Prato retained some local custom but remained under Florentine fiscal and judicial control.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合罗马法传统、城市成文法、行会规章与特别委员会裁断。商业契约、债务关系与财产诉讼高度发达，政治案件则常受非常法庭和派系斗争影响。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Roman-law traditions, municipal statutes, guild regulations, and judgments by special commissions. Commercial contracts, debt relations, and property litigation were highly developed, while political cases were often shaped by extraordinary tribunals and factional struggles.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以直接财产评估税、强制贷款、公债认购、关税、消费税与盐税为主。财政负担在名义上较广泛分配，但富户常通过网络与议价影响实际征收。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation relied on direct assessed property taxes, forced loans, public debt subscriptions, customs, excises, and salt duties. The burden was nominally distributed broadly, but wealthy households often used networks and bargaining power to influence actual collection.

religion.zh: 以拉丁基督教中的罗马天主教为主，城市宗教生活受教区、修会、兄弟会与公民敬礼共同塑造；萨沃纳罗拉运动余波仍影响公共道德与政治语言。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Roman Catholic within Latin Christianity, with urban devotional life shaped by the diocese, religious orders, confraternities, and civic ritual; the aftereffects of the Savonarolan movement still influenced public morality and political language.

philosophy.zh: 人文主义、公民共和思想、基督教道德神学与新柏拉图主义并存，强调古典修辞、历史意识、德性政治与艺术赞助。
philosophy.en: Humanism, civic republican thought, Christian moral theology, and Neoplatonism coexisted, emphasizing classical rhetoric, historical consciousness, virtuous politics, and artistic patronage.

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用拉丁字母；公文与学术作品常以拉丁文书写，民间文学、商业账簿与政治交流广泛使用托斯卡纳意大利语。
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used; official and scholarly works were often written in Latin, while vernacular Tuscan Italian was widely employed in literature, commercial records, and political communication.

culturalAchievements.zh: 佛罗伦萨在绘画、雕塑、建筑、透视法、历史写作、银行簿记、人文教育与印刷传播方面居欧洲前列。米开朗基罗、达·芬奇传统、波提切利余绪以及布鲁内莱斯基建筑遗产持续塑造城市声望。
culturalAchievements.en: Florence stood at the forefront of Europe in painting, sculpture, architecture, perspective, historical writing, banking bookkeeping, humanist education, and the spread of print. The works of Michelangelo, the legacy associated with Leonardo, the afterglow of Botticelli, and Brunelleschi's architectural heritage continued to define the city's prestige.

languageFamily.zh: 印欧语系—意大利语族—托斯卡纳罗曼语支
languageFamily.en: Indo-European family - Italic branch - Tuscan Romance

gdpDescription.zh: 以1500年前后的地中海城市经济标准衡量，佛罗伦萨共和国属于高商业化、高货币化的富裕地区，但其财富主要集中在城市金融、纺织制造与精英家庭，乡村收入远低于首都。
gdpDescription.en: By the standards of Mediterranean urban economies around 1500, the Florentine Republic was a wealthy and highly commercialized, monetized region, though its wealth was concentrated in urban finance, textile manufacturing, and elite households, while the countryside remained significantly poorer than the capital.

mainIndustries.zh: 毛纺织、丝织、染整、银行与汇兑、羊毛进口加工、奢侈品手工业、建筑、艺术品生产、书籍与文具贸易
mainIndustries.en: Wool textiles, silk weaving, dyeing and finishing, banking and exchange, imported wool processing, luxury crafts, construction, art production, and the book and stationery trade

tradeGoods.zh: 高级毛呢、丝绸、染料、成衣、金银器、账册纸张、艺术品、香料转运品、英国羊毛、西班牙羊毛与谷物
tradeGoods.en: Fine woolens, silk, dyestuffs, finished garments, gold and silverware, account books and paper, artworks, re-exported spices, English wool, Spanish wool, and grain

householdWealth.zh: 城市上层家庭拥有房产、乡间别墅、债券、贸易资本与嫁妆基金；中等手工业家庭多有稳定工具与行会联系；乡村家庭财富主要体现为租佃权、牲畜与有限储粮。
householdWealth.en: Upper urban households held town property, country villas, bonds, trade capital, and dowry funds; middling artisan families typically owned tools and guild connections; rural household wealth was mainly expressed in tenancy rights, livestock, and limited grain reserves.

economicSystem.zh: 以市场交换、行会规制、商业信用、公债融资与城市主导的区域农业体系结合而成的前现代商业资本主义经济
economicSystem.en: A premodern commercial-capitalist economy combining market exchange, guild regulation, commercial credit, public debt finance, and an urban-dominated regional agrarian system

source.zh: 公债与财政借款净流入
source.en: Net inflow from public debt and fiscal borrowing

category.zh: 债务利息与旧公债服务
category.en: Debt interest and servicing of old public debt
Public works and maintenance of fortifications

category.zh: 外交、庆典与宗教 civic 支出

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库资金并非全为现银现币，而是由现金储备、可动用税收、短期信用与公债名义价值共同组成。共和国财政信誉优于多数小邦，但长期债务压力持续存在。
treasuryDescription.en: Treasury resources were not held entirely as cash coin but consisted of liquid reserves, assignable revenues, short-term credit, and the nominal value of public debt instruments. The republic's fiscal credit was stronger than that of many small states, though long-term debt remained a persistent burden.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策强调维持信用、控制赤字、在危机时动员富户借款，并以贸易税与属地收入平衡城市支出；稳定时期偏向谨慎保守。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy emphasized preserving credit, limiting deficits, mobilizing loans from wealthy citizens during crises, and balancing urban expenditure through trade duties and territorial revenues; in stable periods it leaned toward caution and conservatism.

description.zh: 由佛罗伦萨公民与属地居民组成，用于城防、守门、巡逻与战时集结，训练程度不一但政治象征意义强。
description.en: Composed of Florentine citizens and inhabitants of subject territories, used for city defense, gate duty, patrols, and wartime mobilization; uneven in training but politically important as a civic force.

description.zh: 主要由意大利与外来职业士兵构成，承担野战、驻防与攻城任务，是共和国常备武力核心。
description.en: Made up chiefly of Italian and foreign professional soldiers, carrying out field operations, garrison duty, and siege service; they formed the core of the republic's standing force.

description.zh: 以雇佣重骑兵和轻骑侦察部队为主，人数有限但在意大利战争中仍具机动价值。
description.en: Consisted mainly of hired heavy cavalry and light scouting troops; limited in number but still valuable for mobility in the Italian Wars.

description.zh: 负责火炮、火药库、城墙加固、桥梁和攻守城技术，反映文艺复兴时期意大利对攻城术的重视。
description.en: Handled cannon, powder magazines, wall strengthening, bridges, and siegecraft, reflecting Renaissance Italy's strong emphasis on artillery and fortification.

description.zh: 驻守比萨及其他托斯卡纳据点，承担地方治安、海陆通道保护与防叛任务。
description.en: Stationed in Pisa and other Tuscan strongpoints, responsible for local order, protection of land and sea routes, and prevention of rebellion.

commanderInChief.zh: 皮耶罗·索德里尼与共和国战争委员会
commanderInChief.en: Piero Soderini and the Republic's War Council

technology.zh: 军队处于冷兵器向火器过渡阶段，广泛使用火炮、火绳枪雏形、弩与长矛方阵；意大利式棱堡体系尚在形成早期，城市工程技术先进。
technology.en: The military was in a transition from cold steel to gunpowder warfare, making broad use of artillery, early handguns, crossbows, and pike formations; the trace italienne was only in an early stage, but urban military engineering was advanced.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自意大利战争中的法国、神圣罗马帝国与西班牙势力干预，以及比萨问题、托斯卡纳属地不稳和内部派系冲突。
threats.en: The principal threats came from intervention by French, Imperial, and Spanish forces during the Italian Wars, as well as the Pisa question, instability in Tuscan subject territories, and internal factional conflict.

populationDescription.zh: 人口包括佛罗伦萨城内居民、阿尔诺河流域市镇居民与托斯卡纳乡村人口。首都密度高、流动人口多，乡间则以佃农、农工和小村社为主。
populationDescription.en: The population included inhabitants of Florence proper, towns in the Arno basin, and the Tuscan countryside. The capital was densely populated and drew many transients, while the countryside was dominated by sharecroppers, farm laborers, and small village communities.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为大商人、银行家、地主与政治家族；中层为公证人、医生、教师、金匠、织工主与熟练工匠；下层包括雇工、学徒、女佣、搬运工与城市贫民；乡村则由地主、佃农、短工和教士共同组成。
socialClasses.en: The upper stratum consisted of great merchants, bankers, landlords, and political families; the middle ranks included notaries, physicians, teachers, goldsmiths, master weavers, and skilled artisans; the lower orders included laborers, apprentices, servants, porters, and the urban poor; the countryside was divided among landlords, sharecroppers, day laborers, and clergy.

allies.zh: 主要依靠与法国的阶段性合作，并在意大利诸邦间寻求均势；与部分教廷人物和地方势力保持务实关系。
allies.en: It relied chiefly on periodic cooperation with France while seeking balance among the Italian states; it also maintained pragmatic ties with elements of the papacy and regional powers.

enemies.zh: 比萨叛离势力、反共和的美第奇支持者，以及在意大利战争中可能威胁佛罗伦萨自主的法国、米兰、教宗国、帝国或西班牙阵营均可成为敌手。
enemies.en: The rebellious forces of Pisa, pro-Medici anti-republican supporters, and any of the French, Milanese, Papal, Imperial, or Spanish camps that threatened Florentine autonomy in the Italian Wars could become enemies.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持共和国独立、避免单一强权控制托斯卡纳、利用金融和使节网络获取安全保证为核心，是典型的意大利均势外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy centered on preserving republican independence, preventing any single great power from dominating Tuscany, and using financial and diplomatic networks to obtain security guarantees; it was a classic form of Italian balance-of-power diplomacy.

keyInnovations.zh: 线性透视、穹顶与城市建筑工程、复式簿记改良、精密钟表与仪器制作、印刷传播、火炮铸造、地图绘制与军事工程学的发展尤为突出。
keyInnovations.en: Linear perspective, dome and urban architectural engineering, improved double-entry bookkeeping, precision clock and instrument making, print dissemination, artillery casting, cartography, and military engineering were especially advanced.

strengths.zh: 金融与商业高度发达，文化威望巨大，行政文书能力强，城市财富集中，工艺与艺术生产领先欧洲，并拥有成熟的外交网络与共和政治传统。
strengths.en: It possessed highly developed finance and commerce, immense cultural prestige, strong administrative literacy, concentrated urban wealth, European-leading craft and artistic production, and mature diplomatic networks rooted in a republican political tradition.

weaknesses.zh: 领土规模有限，粮食与原料依赖外部供应，军事上难与大君主国持久抗衡，派系斗争频繁，财政受公债和战争波动影响较大。
weaknesses.en: Its territory was limited, food and raw materials depended on external supply, its military could not long match major monarchies, factional conflict was frequent, and its finances were vulnerable to debt burdens and wartime volatility.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后的佛罗伦萨在文化与金融上仍极具活力，但在意大利战争的强权夹击下，政治独立将越来越难维持；若外部压力上升，共和体制可能让位于更集中化的家族统治。
outlook.en: Around 1500 Florence remained highly dynamic in culture and finance, but mounting pressure from the great powers of the Italian Wars would make political independence increasingly difficult to preserve; under stronger external pressure, the republic might yield to a more centralized family-based regime.

description.zh: 佛罗伦萨是文艺复兴艺术与金融中心，政治则在共和与寡头之间摇摆。
description.en: Florence was a center of Renaissance art and finance, politically balanced between republicanism and oligarchy.

name.zh: 拉丁基督教文明（教权政体）

rulerTitle.zh: 教宗、罗马主教、教会国君主
rulerTitle.en: Pope, Bishop of Rome, and Sovereign of the Papal States

governmentForm.zh: 以教宗为核心的选举制君主政体兼神权政体，统治教会国并主导西欧拉丁教会体系
governmentForm.en: An elective monarchic theocracy centered on the pope, ruling the Papal States while presiding over the wider Latin Church of Western Christendom

socialStructure.zh: 社会由高级教士、罗马贵族与地方领主、城市公民与行会成员、乡村农民及佃户构成；教会等级与世俗等级相互交织，教廷官僚、修会、法学家和金融家在公共生活中影响显著
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of high clergy, Roman nobles and local lords, urban citizens and guild members, and rural peasants and tenants; ecclesiastical and secular hierarchies overlapped, with curial officials, religious orders, jurists, and financiers exerting strong influence in public life

rulingClass.zh: 教宗、枢机主教团、高级教廷官员、罗马男爵家族以及受封总督和军政长官
rulingClass.en: The pope, the College of Cardinals, senior curial officials, Roman baronial families, and appointed governors and military-administrative captains

succession.zh: 教宗并非世袭，由枢机主教团在秘密会议中选举产生；教会国地方官职则由教宗任命或临时授封
succession.en: The papacy was non-hereditary and filled by election in conclave by the College of Cardinals; offices within the Papal States were appointed or temporarily granted by the pope

structure.zh: 政体由教宗统领，中央依托罗马教廷运作，核心机构包括枢机主教团、宗座内廷、宗座财政机关、宗座法院与各类秘书及文书机关；地方由各城市、总督区与宗座代表管理
structure.en: The polity was headed by the pope and operated through the Roman Curia, with core institutions including the College of Cardinals, papal household, apostolic financial offices, papal courts, and various secretarial and chancery bodies; local territories were governed through cities, legations, and papal governorships

function.zh: 担任教宗顾问、参与重大教务与政务决策，并在教宗去世后负责选举新教宗
function.en: Advised the pope, participated in major ecclesiastical and political decisions, and elected a new pope upon a vacancy

function.zh: 管理教廷财政、税收、采邑收益、公产和战时筹款，并监督部分地方财政征收
function.en: Managed papal finances, taxation, feudal revenues, public assets, and wartime levies, while overseeing portions of local fiscal collection

function.zh: 起草和签发教宗敕令、任命文书、封赐文件及外交函件
function.en: Drafted and issued papal bulls, appointments, grants, and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 审理教会法和部分婚姻、产权、圣职任命等上诉案件，是拉丁教会重要司法机关
function.en: Heard appeals in canon law and certain cases concerning marriage, property, and benefices, serving as a major judicial organ of the Latin Church

function.zh: 处理高级请愿、司法复核和教廷行政裁决事务
function.en: Handled high petitions, judicial review, and curial administrative judgments

function.zh: 在罗马涅、翁布里亚、马尔凯和拉齐奥等地代表教宗实施行政、司法、征税与治安管理
function.en: Represented the pope in Romagna, Umbria, the Marche, Lazio, and other territories, carrying out administration, justice, taxation, and public order

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政依赖教宗任命的总督、使节、城市长官与法官，并与传统自治城市的议会、贵族家族和主教辖区协同或竞争；罗马涅等地的实际控制力常因地方豪强与雇佣军而波动
localAdmin.en: Local administration relied on papal-appointed governors, legates, city rectors, and judges, working alongside or against established urban councils, noble families, and episcopal jurisdictions; effective control, especially in Romagna, often fluctuated because of local magnates and mercenary forces

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系结合教会法、罗马法传统、教宗敕令以及地方习惯法；教会案件由宗座法庭和主教法庭裁断，世俗案件则由地方官与城市法院审理
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined canon law, Roman law traditions, papal decrees, and local customary law; ecclesiastical matters were judged by papal and episcopal courts, while secular disputes were handled by local officials and urban tribunals

taxationSystem.zh: 财政收入来自土地税、关卡与通行税、盐税、城市摊派、司法费用、采邑收益、教会什一税的部分转拨，以及出售圣职与赎罪相关收入；征收效率因地区差异和腐败而不均
taxationSystem.en: Revenue came from land taxes, tolls and transit duties, salt taxes, urban assessments, judicial fees, feudal income, transfers from portions of ecclesiastical tithes, and revenues tied to offices and indulgence-related practices; collection efficiency varied by region and was uneven because of corruption

religion.zh: 罗马天主教为核心信仰，教宗被视为拉丁基督教世界的最高牧首；礼仪、朝圣、圣徒崇拜、修道传统与圣事生活深刻塑造社会秩序
religion.en: Roman Catholicism was the core faith, with the pope regarded as the supreme pastor of Latin Christendom; liturgy, pilgrimage, saintly devotion, monastic traditions, and sacramental life deeply shaped social order

philosophy.zh: 思想上兼具晚期经院哲学与文艺复兴人文主义；托马斯主义、教会法学、古典拉丁文学复兴及基督教人文主义在罗马学术与宫廷文化中并存
