philosophy.en: Intellectual life combined late scholasticism with Renaissance humanism; Thomism, canon-law scholarship, the revival of classical Latin letters, and Christian humanism coexisted in Roman learning and court culture

writingSystem.zh: 主要采用拉丁字母；官方和学术文书以拉丁文为主，行政与日常生活广泛使用意大利各地方言，罗马地区通行中部意大利语变体
writingSystem.en: The Latin alphabet was used; official and scholarly documents were primarily in Latin, while administration and daily life also made broad use of Italian vernaculars, especially central Italian varieties around Rome

culturalAchievements.zh: 1500年前后的罗马是文艺复兴艺术赞助重镇，教廷资助建筑、雕塑、绘画和古典文献整理；圣彼得大殿重建计划、古罗马遗迹研究、礼仪音乐与宗教戏剧均具有代表性
culturalAchievements.en: Rome around 1500 was a major center of Renaissance patronage, with the papacy funding architecture, sculpture, painting, and the recovery of classical texts; plans for the rebuilding of St. Peter’s, study of ancient Roman remains, liturgical music, and religious drama were all notable

languageFamily.zh: 官方学术语言属印欧语系意大利语族中的拉丁语传统；民间主要语言为罗曼语支诸意大利方言
languageFamily.en: The official scholarly language belonged to the Indo-European Italic tradition through Latin; vernacular speech was predominantly in the Romance branch through various Italian dialects

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模中等，以农业、地租、教会财政、朝圣消费、城市手工业和意大利半岛贸易节点功能为基础。与威尼斯、佛罗伦萨等商业强邦相比，教会国并非最富裕，但因教廷集中消费和跨欧教会网络而拥有超出其领土规模的资源动员能力
gdpDescription.en: The economy was moderate in scale, based on agriculture, rents, ecclesiastical finance, pilgrimage spending, urban crafts, and the Papal States’ role in Italian trade routes. Compared with Venice or Florence, the Papal States were not the richest polity, but papal concentrated consumption and a pan-European church network gave them resource-mobilizing power beyond their territorial size

mainIndustries.zh: 粮食农业、葡萄酒与橄榄油生产、牧业、纺织与毛织手工业、建筑业、书籍抄写与印刷、宗教用品制造、朝圣与服务业、教廷行政相关消费
mainIndustries.en: Grain agriculture, wine and olive oil production, pastoralism, textile and woolen crafts, construction, book copying and printing, manufacture of religious goods, pilgrimage and services, and consumption tied to papal administration

tradeGoods.zh: 谷物、葡萄酒、橄榄油、羊毛布、皮革、明矾、宗教器物、书籍、艺术品、香料转运品和奢侈织物
tradeGoods.en: Grain, wine, olive oil, woolen cloth, leather, alum, religious objects, books, artworks, spice transit goods, and luxury textiles

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异显著。罗马和中部城市中的教廷官员、银行家、商人和贵族拥有土地、宅邸、银器与艺术品；多数农村家庭仅持有少量农具、牲畜和居住权，常受赋税和地租压力约束
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was highly unequal. Curial officials, bankers, merchants, and nobles in Rome and central towns held land, houses, plate, and art objects; most rural households possessed only modest tools, livestock, and use rights, and were constrained by taxes and rents

economicSystem.zh: 以农业地租和城市市场交换为基础的前现代混合经济，教会采邑、城市特权、行会制度与跨地区金融网络并存；政治权力与教会财政深度交织
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy based on agrarian rents and urban market exchange, combining ecclesiastical lordship, urban privileges, guild structures, and interregional financial networks; political power was deeply intertwined with church finance

source.en: Land Taxes and Local Direct Taxes
Customs, Transit Duties, and Salt Taxes

source.zh: 教会收益与圣职相关收入
司法费用、罚金与采邑收入
source.en: Judicial Fees, Fines, and Feudal Revenues

category.zh: 建筑、艺术赞助与宗教工程
category.en: Building, Artistic Patronage, and Religious Works

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非始终充裕，而是在高额常规收入与沉重宫廷、军事、裙带与建筑支出之间波动。教廷可借助银行家、预征收益与出售职务来弥补短缺，但长期财政纪律并不稳固
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not consistently abundant, fluctuating between high ordinary revenues and heavy courtly, military, nepotistic, and building expenditures. The papacy could bridge shortfalls through bankers, revenue anticipations, and office sales, but long-term fiscal discipline was not firm

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持教廷权威、资助战争与宫廷声望工程，短期筹资能力强，但对临时税、借贷和职务出售依赖较深，存在结构性浪费
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining papal authority and funding war and prestige projects; short-term fundraising capacity was strong, but dependence on extraordinary taxes, borrowing, and office sales created structural waste

description.zh: 负责罗马城、要塞和教廷核心机构的守卫，由本地兵、外籍卫队和受薪士兵组成
description.en: Protected the city of Rome, fortresses, and core papal institutions, composed of local troops, foreign guards, and paid soldiers

description.zh: 为教会国野战主力，含意大利步兵及部分外籍兵员，装备长枪、弩和早期火器
description.en: Formed the main field force of the Papal States, including Italian infantry and some foreign soldiers equipped with pikes, crossbows, and early firearms

description.zh: 由贵族随从、职业骑士和受雇骑兵组成，用于机动打击、护卫和威慑地方领主
description.en: Consisted of noble retinues, professional horsemen, and hired cavalry used for mobile striking, escort duty, and intimidation of local lords

name.en: Local Militia and Urban Defense Forces

description.zh: 由城市与乡区征集，平时训练有限，战时负责守城、补给与治安
description.en: Raised from towns and rural districts, with limited peacetime training; in war they handled garrison duty, supply, and security

commanderInChief.zh: 教宗亚历山大六世，实际军事指挥多委托教宗任命的总司令与孔多铁埃
commanderInChief.en: Pope Alexander VI, though practical command was often delegated to papal captains-general and condottieri

technology.zh: 军事技术处于意大利战争时代水平，使用火绳枪和火炮，但仍高度依赖雇佣军传统、筑城体系和骑兵声望；在重炮攻城和防御工事方面受意大利半岛军事实践影响明显
technology.en: Military technology was at the level of the Italian Wars era, employing arquebuses and artillery while still relying heavily on mercenary traditions, fortifications, and cavalry prestige; siege artillery and defensive works were strongly shaped by Italian military practice

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自意大利半岛列强竞争、罗马涅地方领主叛服无常、法国与西班牙对意大利事务的军事干预，以及与神圣罗马帝国势力和意大利诸邦关系变化
threats.en: Major threats came from competition among Italian powers, the unstable submission of Romagnol lords, French and Spanish military intervention in Italian affairs, and shifts in relations with Imperial and Italian states

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于拉齐奥、翁布里亚、马尔凯和罗马涅的农业区与中小城市。罗马因教廷、朝圣和建筑活动吸引人口，但整体仍以农村居民为主
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in the agrarian zones and medium and small towns of Lazio, Umbria, the Marche, and Romagna. Rome attracted people through the papacy, pilgrimage, and building activity, but the population remained predominantly rural overall

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括教宗家族、枢机、高级教士、罗马与地方贵族、富商和银行家；中层为公证人、法学家、行会师傅、下级教士和城市商人；下层为手工业者、雇工、农民、佃户与贫民。修士、修女和大学学者构成具有特殊社会地位的群体
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the papal family, cardinals, high clergy, Roman and provincial nobles, wealthy merchants, and bankers; the middle strata included notaries, jurists, guild masters, lower clergy, and urban traders; the lower strata comprised artisans, laborers, peasants, tenants, and the poor. Monks, nuns, and university scholars formed socially distinctive groups

allies.zh: 无固定长期盟友，外交上常在西班牙、法国、威尼斯、米兰、佛罗伦萨与那不勒斯之间周旋；与西班牙王室在博尔吉亚时期关系尤为重要
allies.en: There were no fixed long-term allies; diplomatically the papacy maneuvered among Spain, France, Venice, Milan, Florence, and Naples, with ties to the Spanish monarchy especially important during the Borgia period

enemies.zh: 主要敌对力量并非恒定国家，而是任何威胁教会国领土完整或教宗自主性的势力，包括意大利叛乱贵族、敌对城邦以及在意大利半岛扩张的法国或其他外国军队
enemies.en: Its principal enemies were not constant states but any forces threatening the territorial integrity of the Papal States or papal autonomy, including rebellious Italian nobles, hostile city-states, and foreign armies such as the French when expanding in Italy

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策核心在于维持教宗在拉丁基督教世界的最高仲裁地位，同时巩固教会国在中意大利的领土控制。教廷通过任命使节、缔结短期同盟、发布教会裁决和运用婚姻与封赏政治来平衡列强
foreignPolicy.en: The core of foreign policy was to preserve the pope’s supreme arbitral position in Latin Christendom while consolidating territorial control in central Italy. The papacy balanced great powers through legates, short-term alliances, ecclesiastical rulings, and the politics of marriage and patronage

keyInnovations.zh: 印刷术传播、古典建筑学复兴、线性透视绘画、火炮与手持火器应用、教会行政中的文书标准化、地图制作与人文主义文本校勘
keyInnovations.en: The spread of printing, revival of classical architecture, linear-perspective painting, use of artillery and handheld firearms, standardization of documentary practice in church administration, cartography, and humanist textual criticism

strengths.zh: 最大优势在于其双重权威：既是西方拉丁教会的宗教中心，又是意大利半岛的重要世俗政权。罗马的象征地位、跨欧洲教会网络、财政汲取能力以及文艺复兴文化赞助，使其在外交和文化上远超一般中等国家
strengths.en: Its greatest strength lay in its dual authority: it was both the religious center of the Western Latin Church and a major secular power in Italy. Rome’s symbolic standing, the pan-European church network, fiscal extraction capacity, and Renaissance cultural patronage gave it diplomatic and cultural weight far beyond that of an ordinary medium-sized state

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于领土控制碎片化、对雇佣军依赖严重、地方豪族势力顽强，以及财政和任官中普遍存在裙带、腐败与短期主义。教宗选举制也使政策连续性较弱
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were fragmented territorial control, heavy reliance on mercenaries, the resilience of local magnates, and widespread nepotism, corruption, and short-termism in finance and officeholding. The elective nature of the papacy also weakened long-term policy continuity

outlook.zh: 1500年的局势总体稳定但隐含风险。若能借意大利战争重建对罗马涅与中部地区的有效统治，教会国将增强；若外来列强深入干预、财政失衡并激化教会内部批评，则其宗教威望和世俗统治都可能遭遇严重挑战
outlook.en: The situation in 1500 was broadly stable but contained major risks. If the papacy could use the Italian Wars to rebuild effective control over Romagna and central Italy, the Papal States would be strengthened; if foreign powers intervened more deeply, fiscal imbalance worsened, and criticism within the Church intensified, both its religious prestige and secular rule could face serious challenges

description.zh: 教皇国既是宗教中心，也是意大利半岛重要世俗政权。
description.en: The Papal States were both the center of Latin Christendom and a major secular power in Italy.

name.zh: 马格里布文明（哈夫斯苏丹国）

governmentForm.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰君主制为基础的世袭苏丹国，结合宫廷官僚、地方总督与城市精英协商治理。
governmentForm.en: A hereditary Sunni Islamic sultanate combining court bureaucracy, provincial governors, and negotiated rule with urban elites.

socialStructure.zh: 社会由王室与宫廷军政精英、宗教学者与法官、城市商人和手工业者、乡村农民、部族群体以及奴隶与依附人口构成；沿海城市社会比内陆更商业化与多元。
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the ruling household and military-administrative elite, religious scholars and judges, urban merchants and artisans, rural peasants, tribal groups, and enslaved or dependent populations; coastal cities were more commercialized and diverse than the interior.

rulingClass.zh: 哈夫斯王室、宫廷军官、总督、税务官员、马立克学派乌里玛及与国家合作的城市商人家族。
rulingClass.en: The Hafsid royal house, court officers, governors, fiscal officials, Maliki ulama, and urban merchant families allied with the state.

succession.zh: 原则上为王朝世袭，由苏丹家族内部继承；实际常受宫廷派系、军队支持与宗教精英承认影响。
succession.en: In principle dynastic and hereditary within the ruling family, though in practice shaped by court factions, military backing, and recognition from religious elites.

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹为核心，下设宫廷书记机构、财政征收体系、司法与宗教管理体系以及省级总督网络；统治依赖城市行政与对部族领袖的协商控制。
structure.en: The central government was centered on the sultan, with chancery offices, a fiscal collection system, judicial-religious administration, and a network of provincial governors; rule depended on urban administration and negotiated control over tribal leaders.

function.zh: 起草诏令、处理外交文书、保管档案并协调中央行政。
function.en: Drafted decrees, handled diplomatic correspondence, kept archives, and coordinated central administration.

function.zh: 征收土地税、关税、市场税与属地贡赋，管理国库与支出。
function.en: Collected land taxes, customs duties, market taxes, and provincial tribute, and managed the treasury and expenditures.

function.zh: 监督卡迪、清真寺与宗教捐赠，维持以马立克法学为主的司法秩序。
function.en: Supervised qadis, mosques, and religious endowments, maintaining a judicial order primarily based on Maliki jurisprudence.

function.zh: 管理地方总督、城防工事、港口治安与驿站交通。
function.en: Managed provincial governors, urban fortifications, port security, and communications routes.

function.zh: 监管突尼斯、比塞大、斯法克斯等港口的贸易、征税与商人往来。
function.en: Oversaw trade, customs, and merchant traffic in ports such as Tunis, Bizerte, and Sfax.

localAdmin.zh: 地方由总督、城镇长官、税吏与卡迪共同管理；沿海城镇行政较完善，内陆则更依赖与部族酋长、地方宗教领袖和堡寨指挥官的合作。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was shared by governors, town officials, tax collectors, and qadis; coastal towns had more developed administration, while the interior relied more heavily on cooperation with tribal chiefs, local religious leaders, and fortress commanders.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派马立克法学为主，辅以苏丹敕令、市场监管条例和地方习惯法；商业纠纷多由卡迪与行会、商人共同调解。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on Sunni Maliki jurisprudence, supplemented by sultanic decrees, market regulations, and local custom; commercial disputes were often mediated by qadis together with guilds and merchants.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要来自农业土地税、港口关税、市场税、牧业征收、非正式贡赋及特许经营收入；海关税对国家财政尤为重要。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation came chiefly from agricultural land taxes, port customs, market dues, pastoral levies, informal tribute, and concession revenues; customs duties were especially important to state finance.

religion.zh: 以逊尼派伊斯兰教为主，法学上以马立克学派占优势，并受苏非派、圣徒崇拜和地方宗教传统影响；犹太人与少量基督徒商人亦活动于城市中。
religion.en: Religion was predominantly Sunni Islam, with Maliki jurisprudence dominant and significant influence from Sufi networks, saint veneration, and local devotional traditions; Jewish communities and small numbers of Christian merchants were also present in cities.

philosophy.zh: 思想生活以伊斯兰法学、神学、阿拉伯文文辞学和实用政治伦理为核心，同时吸收安达卢斯与更广泛马格里布学术传统。
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic jurisprudence, theology, Arabic literary culture, and practical political ethics, while drawing on Andalusi and broader Maghrebi scholarly traditions.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为阿拉伯字母，用于行政、公文、宗教与文学；柏柏尔语言多以口传为主，商贸社群中亦可见希伯来文与拉丁文文书。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Arabic script, used for administration, official documents, religion, and literature; Berber languages were more often transmitted orally, while Hebrew and Latin documents also appeared in mercantile communities.

culturalAchievements.zh: 突尼斯及凯鲁万延续了马格里布城市学术与建筑传统，体现在清真寺、宗教学校、苏非团体、书法、装饰艺术、制陶、织造与跨地中海商业文化之中。
culturalAchievements.en: Tunis and Kairouan sustained the urban scholarly and architectural traditions of the Maghreb, visible in mosques, madrasas, Sufi institutions, calligraphy, decorative arts, ceramics, textiles, and a vibrant trans-Mediterranean commercial culture.

languageFamily.zh: 官方高文化语言属闪米特语系阿拉伯语；民间同时使用阿拉伯方言与阿非罗-亚细亚语系中的柏柏尔诸语。
languageFamily.en: The official high-cultural language was Arabic of the Semitic family; vernacular life also included Arabic dialects and Berber languages of the Afroasiatic family.

gdpDescription.zh: 经济规模在北非属中上水平，核心优势在于农业腹地、橄榄油与谷物生产、手工业和地中海转口贸易；但其财政与商业繁荣受海上竞争、地方部族压力与港口控制能力影响。
gdpDescription.en: The economy ranked in the upper middle range for North Africa, with strengths in its agricultural hinterland, olive oil and grain production, crafts, and Mediterranean entrepot trade; however, fiscal and commercial prosperity was affected by maritime competition, tribal pressures, and the state's ability to control ports.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄油、畜牧、皮革加工、纺织、陶器、金属手工业、港口贸易与海运服务。
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive oil, pastoralism, leatherworking, textiles, pottery, metal crafts, port trade, and shipping services.

tradeGoods.zh: 出口谷物、橄榄油、羊毛、皮革、蜡、珊瑚、纺织品与北非马匹；进口香料、细布、金属制品、木材、奴隶以及来自撒哈拉以南的黄金。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included grain, olive oil, wool, leather, wax, coral, textiles, and North African horses; imports included spices, fine cloth, metalwares, timber, slaves, and gold from trans-Saharan regions.

householdWealth.zh: 城市商人和宗教学者家庭可积累房产、店铺与金银储蓄，乡村家庭财富主要体现为土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和宗族网络；整体财富分布不均，但城市中产与商贸阶层较活跃。
householdWealth.en: Urban merchant and scholarly households could accumulate property, shops, and precious-metal savings, while rural household wealth was mainly held in land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and kinship ties; wealth was unevenly distributed overall, though an active urban middle stratum existed.

economicSystem.zh: 经济体制为伊斯兰君主制下的农业—商业混合经济，国家通过税收、关税、港口监管和授予商人特权介入市场，但私人商贸与地方网络仍占主导。
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an agrarian-commercial mixed economy under an Islamic monarchy, in which the state intervened through taxes, customs, port supervision, and merchant privileges, while private trade and local networks remained dominant.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库保持中等偏充足水平，足以支持城防、舰队和宫廷开支，但仍容易因歉收、海盗活动、港口贸易衰退或王朝内斗而受压。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury stood at a moderately healthy level, sufficient to support fortifications, fleet maintenance, and court expenses, but it remained vulnerable to poor harvests, piracy, port decline, or dynastic conflict.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏审慎，重视维持港口税收、稳定粮价和保障军费；在地方控制减弱时，国家常以包税、特许或临时摊派方式弥补收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was relatively cautious, prioritizing port revenue, stable grain prices, and military funding; when local control weakened, the state often relied on tax farming, concessions, or extraordinary levies to fill revenue gaps.

description.zh: 由苏丹直属骑兵、贵族随从与精锐卫队组成，用于野战、护卫与快速镇压叛乱。
description.en: Composed of the sultan's direct cavalry, noble retainers, and elite guards, used for field warfare, protection, and rapid suppression of revolts.

description.zh: 主要负责城墙、堡垒、港口和重要道路防守，包含长矛兵、弓箭手、弩手及少量火器兵。
description.en: Responsible mainly for defending walls, forts, ports, and major roads, including spearmen, archers, crossbowmen, and a small number of firearm troops.

description.zh: 在战时由内陆与边地部族提供，擅长机动作战、侦察与荒漠地带行动，但忠诚度依赖赏赐与政治关系。
description.en: Supplied in wartime by inland and frontier tribes, effective in mobile warfare, scouting, and desert operations, though loyalty depended on patronage and political relations.

description.zh: 规模有限，主要用于护航、缉私、海盗防御与港口控制，而非远洋海军决战。
description.en: Limited in scale and intended mainly for convoy protection, anti-smuggling, anti-piracy operations, and port control rather than blue-water naval battle.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹穆罕默德四世
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Muhammad IV

technology.zh: 军备以传统骑兵、城防工事和港口防御为主，已知并有限采用火药武器与火炮，但尚未形成像奥斯曼那样高度火器化的军制。
technology.en: Military technology remained centered on traditional cavalry, fortifications, and port defenses, with limited use of gunpowder weapons and artillery, but without the highly firearm-oriented system of the Ottomans.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自西方马格里布竞争政权、部族叛乱、地中海海盗与欧洲海上势力扩张，以及来自东方奥斯曼影响力逐步逼近的战略压力。
threats.en: The main threats came from rival western Maghrebi polities, tribal revolts, Mediterranean piracy and expanding European naval powers, as well as the strategic pressure of growing Ottoman influence from the east.

populationDescription.zh: 人口集中于突尼斯、凯鲁万、沿海港口和较肥沃的农业地带，内陆与南部地区较为稀疏；社会由阿拉伯化城市人口、柏柏尔部族、安达卢斯移民后裔、犹太社群和奴隶人口共同构成。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in Tunis, Kairouan, coastal ports, and the more fertile agricultural zones, while the interior and southern regions were sparsely settled; society included Arabized urban populations, Berber tribes, descendants of Andalusi migrants, Jewish communities, and enslaved peoples.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括王室、军政贵显、乌里玛和大商人；中层包括工匠、店主、小地主、文书和宗教学生；下层包括农民、牧民、城市劳工、港口杂役、奴隶与依附人口。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal house, military-administrative notables, ulama, and great merchants; the middle strata included artisans, shopkeepers, small landholders, scribes, and religious students; the lower strata included peasants, herders, urban laborers, dock workers, slaves, and dependents.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的固定同盟，更多依靠与意大利海上城邦、阿拉贡及周边马格里布势力的临时贸易与政治安排；在伊斯兰世界中与埃及、马什里克商路保持联系。
allies.en: There were no fixed long-term allies in the modern sense; instead the state relied on temporary commercial and political arrangements with Italian maritime cities, Aragon, and neighboring Maghrebi powers, while maintaining trade links with Egypt and the wider Mashriq.

enemies.zh: 潜在敌对者包括扎扬王朝、瓦塔斯王朝等西马格里布竞争者、活跃于海上的伊比利亚势力，以及可能干预东部北非事务的奥斯曼力量。
enemies.en: Potential enemies included western Maghrebi rivals such as the Zayyanids and Wattasids, Iberian powers active at sea, and Ottoman forces that might intervene in eastern North African affairs.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维持贸易航道、平衡基督教海权与邻近穆斯林政权、争取港口收益和避免被更强帝国吞并为核心，具有高度务实性。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was highly pragmatic, centered on maintaining trade routes, balancing Christian naval powers and neighboring Muslim states, securing port revenues, and avoiding absorption by stronger empires.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术包括成熟的地中海航海术、港口仓储与税关管理、灌溉农业、城市防御工事、纺织与皮革加工，以及逐步引入的火药武器与火炮铸造技术。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies included mature Mediterranean navigation, port warehousing and customs administration, irrigated agriculture, urban fortification, textile and leather processing, and the gradual adoption of gunpowder weapons and artillery casting.

strengths.zh: 国家的优势在于突尼斯作为地中海贸易节点的地理位置、成熟的伊斯兰城市文化、较强的港口财政收入以及连接撒哈拉、马格里布和欧洲市场的商业网络。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in Tunis's geographic position as a Mediterranean trade node, a mature Islamic urban culture, strong port-based revenues, and commercial networks linking the Sahara, the Maghreb, and European markets.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于王朝控制力受地方部族与地区分裂制约，军事现代化有限，财政对海关收入依赖较高，且在奥斯曼与伊比利亚扩张夹缝中战略空间逐渐缩小。
weaknesses.en: Weaknesses included dynastic control constrained by tribal power and regional fragmentation, limited military modernization, high dependence on customs revenue, and a shrinking strategic space between Ottoman and Iberian expansion.

outlook.zh: 1500年前后的哈夫斯政权仍属稳定且具区域重要性，但长期前景并不稳固；若不能强化军制、巩固内陆控制并适应地中海权力格局变化，其独立性将在16世纪面临更大挑战。
outlook.en: Around 1500 the Hafsid regime remained stable and regionally important, but its long-term outlook was uncertain; unless it strengthened its military system, consolidated inland control, and adapted to changing Mediterranean power politics, its independence would face greater challenges in the sixteenth century.

description.zh: 哈夫斯王朝仍统治伊夫里基亚，是中西地中海间的重要马格里布国家。
description.en: The Hafsids still ruled Ifriqiya and remained an important Maghrebi state between the central and western Mediterranean.

governmentForm.zh: 伊斯兰君主制，结合马赫赞宫廷政治、城市官僚与地方部族协商统治
governmentForm.en: Islamic monarchy combining makhzen court politics, urban bureaucracy, and negotiated rule with provincial tribes

socialStructure.zh: 社会由苏丹与宫廷精英、宗教学者、城市商人和工匠、乡村部族、农民以及奴隶和依附民组成；沿海城市与内陆部族社会差异明显。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the sultan and court elite, religious scholars, urban merchants and artisans, rural tribes, peasants, and slaves and dependents; strong contrasts existed between coastal and inland tribal society.

rulingClass.zh: 瓦塔斯王朝宗室、宫廷军政官员、非斯与其他城市的乌里玛和大商人
rulingClass.en: The Wattasid royal house, court military-administrative officials, and the ulama and great merchants of Fez and other cities

succession.zh: 王朝世袭，但常受宗室竞争、地方军事实力与宗教威望影响，继承并不完全稳定
succession.en: Dynastic hereditary succession, but often shaped by princely rivalry, provincial military power, and religious prestige, making succession less than fully stable

structure.zh: 中央政府以苏丹宫廷为核心，依靠维齐尔、财政官、法官、军政总管和地方总督运作；实际控制力在北部城市与交通要道较强，在南部和山区更多依赖盟约与承认关系。
structure.en: The central government was centered on the sultan's court and operated through viziers, fiscal officials, judges, military administrators, and provincial governors; effective control was strongest in northern cities and strategic routes, while in the south and mountains it relied more on alliances and acknowledgment.

function.zh: 负责政令起草、宫廷协调、对外接待和高层任命
function.en: Handled drafting decrees, court coordination, foreign reception, and high appointments

function.zh: 征收土地税、市场税、关税和属地贡赋，管理国库与拨款
function.en: Collected land taxes, market levies, customs duties, and provincial tribute, and managed the treasury and allocations

function.zh: 监督卡迪法院、宗教捐赠、教法执行与学术机构
function.en: Oversaw qadi courts, religious endowments, implementation of Islamic law, and scholarly institutions

function.zh: 统筹卫戍部队、骑兵召集、城防维修和军需供应
function.en: Coordinated garrison troops, cavalry mobilization, fortress maintenance, and military supply

function.zh: 联络总督、地方长官与部族首领，传递命令并监督省级事务
function.en: Liaised with governors, local officials, and tribal chiefs, transmitted orders, and supervised provincial affairs

localAdmin.zh: 地方由总督、城镇长官、卡迪和税务代理共同治理；在部族地区，苏丹政权往往通过盟约、宗教领袖和定期征发维持影响力。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was conducted by governors, town officials, qadis, and tax agents; in tribal zones, the sultanate often maintained influence through agreements, religious leaders, and periodic levies.

legalSystem.zh: 以马立克学派伊斯兰教法为核心，辅以苏丹敕令、行政惯例和部族习惯法；城市司法更制度化，乡村与山地地区则更依赖地方调解。
legalSystem.en: The legal system centered on Maliki Islamic law, supplemented by sultanic decrees, administrative practice, and tribal custom; justice was more institutionalized in cities, while rural and mountain regions relied more on local mediation.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收主要包括土地税、农产征收、商税、市场税、关税和对部族与附属地区的贡赋；由于王权衰弱，实际征收不均且常被地方势力截留。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation consisted mainly of land taxes, agricultural dues, commercial taxes, market levies, customs duties, and tribute from tribes and dependent areas; because royal authority was weakening, actual collection was uneven and often intercepted by local powers.

religion.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教为主，奉行马立克学派，苏菲教团和圣裔家族在社会与政治上具有重要影响
religion.en: Predominantly Sunni Islam of the Maliki school, with Sufi orders and sharifian lineages exerting major social and political influence

philosophy.zh: 思想上以伊斯兰法学、神学、苏菲修行与阿拉伯-柏柏尔政治伦理为主，重视学术传承、圣贤权威与社会秩序
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic jurisprudence, theology, Sufi spirituality, and Arab-Berber political ethics, valuing scholarly transmission, saintly authority, and social order

writingSystem.zh: 主要使用阿拉伯字母书写阿拉伯语，也用于记录部分柏柏尔语宗教与行政内容
writingSystem.en: Primarily used the Arabic script for Arabic, and also for some religious and administrative use of Berber languages

culturalAchievements.zh: 非斯的宗教教育和手抄书传统延续繁荣，卡拉维因等学术机构保持声望；建筑上延续马格里布-安达卢斯风格，体现在清真寺、经学院、喷泉、中庭和细木灰泥装饰；皮革、金属器、纺织和陶艺工艺发达。
culturalAchievements.en: Religious learning and manuscript culture remained vibrant in Fez, and institutions such as al-Qarawiyyin retained prestige; architecture continued the Maghrebi-Andalusi style in mosques, madrasas, fountains, courtyards, and carved wood and stucco decoration; leatherwork, metalwork, textiles, and ceramics were highly developed.

languageFamily.zh: 以阿拉伯语和柏柏尔语为主，分别属于闪米特语族和阿非罗-亚细亚语系柏柏尔语支
languageFamily.en: Arabic and Berber predominated, belonging respectively to the Semitic branch and the Berber branch of the Afro-Asiatic language family

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以农业、畜牧、跨撒哈拉贸易、城市手工业和区域转口贸易为基础，但受王权衰弱、葡萄牙占据海岸据点以及内地割据影响，整体处于停滞并带有衰退趋势。
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on agriculture, pastoralism, trans-Saharan exchange, urban crafts, and regional entrepôt trade, but weakening royal authority, Portuguese coastal enclaves, and inland fragmentation produced stagnation with a declining trend.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、橄榄与果园经营、牧羊牧马、皮革加工、纺织、金属工艺、陶器制造、糖和染料贸易、跨撒哈拉商队运输
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, olive and orchard agriculture, sheep and horse raising, leatherworking, textiles, metal crafts, pottery, trade in sugar and dyestuffs, and trans-Saharan caravan transport

tradeGoods.zh: 皮革、羊毛织物、铜器、陶器、糖、蜡、盐、马匹、谷物，以及来自撒哈拉以南的黄金、奴隶和象牙；进口金属制品、纺织品和部分奢侈消费品
tradeGoods.en: Leather, wool textiles, copperware, ceramics, sugar, wax, salt, horses, and grain, along with gold, slaves, and ivory from sub-Saharan routes; imports included metal goods, textiles, and selected luxury items

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富有限，多以土地使用权、牲畜、粮食储备和手工工具为主；城市富商和宗教基金掌握较多流动资本与不动产。
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was modest and consisted largely of land-use rights, livestock, grain stores, and craft tools; urban merchants and religious endowments held greater liquid capital and real estate.

economicSystem.zh: 以农业和部族生产为基础的前近代混合经济，城市市场、伊斯兰契约、宗教基金和宫廷征收共同运作，国家控制能力有限。
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy based on agriculture and tribal production, with urban markets, Islamic contracting, religious endowments, and court taxation operating together under limited state control.

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库规模中等但不稳，受战争、沿海贸易受损和地方留税影响较大；现金储备不足以长期支持大规模征讨。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderate in size but unstable, heavily affected by war, impaired coastal trade, and local retention of taxes; cash reserves were insufficient to sustain major campaigns for long.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持宫廷、守卫北部核心地区并以临时征收弥补赤字，但这种做法加重地方不满并削弱长期税基。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized maintaining the court, defending the northern core, and covering deficits through extraordinary exactions, but this increased local discontent and weakened the long-term tax base.

name.zh: 苏丹近卫与城市卫戍军

description.zh: 负责保卫非斯等核心城市、宫廷与要塞，包含步兵、弓弩手及早期火器使用者。
description.en: Protected Fez and other core cities, the court, and fortresses, including infantry, archers or crossbowmen, and early firearm users.

description.zh: 由阿拉伯与柏柏尔部族按召集出征，是机动主力，适合袭扰、追击和区域控制，但忠诚度依赖政治联盟。
description.en: Raised from Arab and Berber tribes, these formed the main mobile arm, effective for raiding, pursuit, and regional control, but their loyalty depended on political alliances.

description.zh: 来自城镇和乡村的临时兵员，用于守城、运输和地方作战，训练与装备参差不齐。
description.en: Temporary troops drawn from towns and countryside for garrisoning, transport, and local fighting, with uneven training and equipment.

description.zh: 人数不多，主要操作火炮并参与城防工程，在面对葡萄牙沿海堡垒时作用重要但技术仍有限。
description.en: Small in number, they operated artillery and worked on fortifications; important against Portuguese coastal strongholds, though still limited in technical sophistication.

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹穆罕默德·谢赫
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Muhammad al-Shaykh

technology.zh: 军队以传统骑兵与城防体系为主，已接触和有限采用火药武器、火炮和火绳枪，但总体装备和组织逊于奥斯曼和伊比利亚沿海强权。
technology.en: The military was centered on traditional cavalry and fortress warfare, with some adoption of gunpowder weapons, artillery, and arquebuses, but overall equipment and organization lagged behind Ottoman and Iberian coastal powers.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自葡萄牙在大西洋沿岸的据点、地方诸侯和部族叛离、南方沙里夫势力崛起以及财政衰弱导致的军队离心。
threats.en: The main threats came from Portuguese Atlantic enclaves, local magnates and tribal defections, the rise of southern sharifian forces, and military fragmentation caused by fiscal weakness.

populationDescription.zh: 人口约三百万，主要集中于非斯、北部平原、山麓农区和若干商路绿洲；沿海部分地区因葡萄牙据点和战争受扰，人口流动较明显。
populationDescription.en: The population of about three million was concentrated in Fez, the northern plains, piedmont farming zones, and several oasis trade corridors; some coastal areas saw notable disruption and movement because of Portuguese enclaves and warfare.

socialClasses.zh: 上层包括苏丹宗室、宫廷官员、宗教学者、沙里夫家族和大商人；中层为城市工匠、店主、小商人与低级书吏；下层为农民、牧民、雇工、奴隶和依附民。部族社会内部也按血缘、武力和宗教威望形成等级。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata included the royal house, court officials, religious scholars, sharifian families, and major merchants; the middle strata comprised urban artisans, shopkeepers, petty traders, and lower scribes; the lower strata consisted of peasants, pastoralists, laborers, slaves, and dependents. Tribal society also formed hierarchies around lineage, force, and religious prestige.

allies.zh: 无稳定强力盟友；与部分北非穆斯林城市、撒哈拉商路领主和地方部族维持务实关系
allies.en: No stable powerful allies; maintained pragmatic relations with some North African Muslim cities, Saharan trade lords, and local tribes

enemies.zh: 葡萄牙是最直接的外部敌手；同时面临南方萨阿迪派系和国内分裂势力的挑战
enemies.en: Portugal was the most immediate external enemy; the polity also faced challenges from the southern Saadian faction and internal divisive forces

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交以守成为主，寻求维护北部核心地区、保全贸易线路并限制伊比利亚势力扩张；在实际操作上常以谈判、停战、赎俘和临时联盟应对危机。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy was mainly defensive, seeking to preserve the northern core, protect trade routes, and limit Iberian expansion; in practice it often relied on negotiation, truces, ransoming, and temporary alliances to manage crises.

keyInnovations.zh: 延续成熟的灌溉农业、皮革鞣制、安达卢斯式建筑工艺、手抄书与伊斯兰学术传统，并有限吸收火药武器和城防炮术；技术优势主要体现在工艺和城市文化，而非国家军事革新。
keyInnovations.en: The civilization sustained mature irrigated agriculture, leather tanning, Andalusi architectural craft, manuscript production, and Islamic scholarly traditions, while only partially absorbing gunpowder weapons and artillery fortification methods; its strengths lay more in craftsmanship and urban culture than in state military innovation.

strengths.zh: 拥有非斯等历史文化中心、跨撒哈拉商路联系、深厚的伊斯兰法学与学术传统，以及可动员的部族骑兵资源。
strengths.en: It possessed historic cultural centers such as Fez, links to trans-Saharan trade, deep Islamic legal and scholarly traditions, and mobilizable tribal cavalry resources.

weaknesses.zh: 中央权威衰弱、财政赤字、地方离心、海岸受葡萄牙压迫、火器和行政整合不足，使王朝难以恢复全国性控制。
weaknesses.en: Weak central authority, fiscal deficits, provincial fragmentation, Portuguese pressure on the coast, and inadequate firearms and administrative integration made it difficult for the dynasty to restore kingdom-wide control.

outlook.zh: 短期前景不佳。若瓦塔斯王朝无法重整财政、吸纳南方宗教与军事力量并有效应对葡萄牙威胁，其统治很可能继续衰退并被更具动员力的新势力取代。
outlook.en: The short-term outlook was poor. Unless the Wattasids could rebuild finances, incorporate southern religious and military forces, and respond effectively to the Portuguese threat, their rule was likely to continue declining and be replaced by a more mobilizing new power.

description.zh: 瓦塔斯王朝控制摩洛哥北部，但权力受地方势力与葡萄牙沿海据点挑战。
description.en: The Wattasids controlled northern Morocco, but their power was challenged by local rivals and Portuguese coastal enclaves.

name.zh: 埃塞俄比亚基督教文明

governmentForm.zh: 君主制帝国，兼具宫廷巡幸与地方贵族统治特征
governmentForm.en: Monarchical empire characterized by an itinerant court and regional noble rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会以皇帝、王族与大贵族为上层，教会与修道院构成重要精神与经济力量，其下为地方军政贵族、自由农民、佃农、工匠、商人及少量奴仆人口。高原基督教核心区在政治上占主导地位，边缘地区通过贡赋、封臣关系与军事镇压被整合。
socialStructure.en: Society was headed by the emperor, royal kin, and great nobles, while the Church and monasteries formed a major spiritual and economic force. Beneath them stood provincial military-administrative nobles, free peasants, tenants, artisans, merchants, and a smaller servile population. The Christian highland core dominated politically, while peripheral regions were integrated through tribute, vassalage, and military coercion.

rulingClass.zh: 所罗门王朝皇室、阿姆哈拉与提格雷高地贵族、世俗军政长官以及埃塞俄比亚正教高级教士
rulingClass.en: The Solomonic royal house, Amhara and Tigrayan highland nobility, secular military-administrative lords, and senior clergy of the Ethiopian Orthodox Church

succession.zh: 王位原则上由所罗门王朝男性成员继承，但实际常受宫廷联盟、贵族支持与军事控制影响，并非严格长子继承
succession.en: The throne was in principle inherited within the male Solomonic line, but in practice succession was often shaped by court alliances, noble backing, and military control rather than strict primogeniture.

structure.zh: 帝国政体以皇帝为核心，依靠王室亲族、封地贵族、地方总督、军政长官及教会网络共同治理。由于王廷长期巡行，高层决策在王家营地中完成，地方行政则通过省级领主、贡赋体系和教会地产维系。
structure.en: The imperial system centered on the emperor and operated through royal kin, landed nobles, provincial governors, military-administrative commanders, and church networks. Because the court was largely itinerant, high-level decisions were made in the royal camp, while local administration was sustained through provincial lords, tribute arrangements, and ecclesiastical estates.

function.zh: 管理皇帝随行宫廷、觐见、诏令、营地供应、王室财产与礼仪
function.en: Managed the emperor’s itinerant court, audiences, decrees, camp provisioning, royal property, and ceremony

function.zh: 征收土地税、实物贡赋、附庸贡物与战利品，监督仓储与分配
function.en: Collected land taxes, payments in kind, tributary deliveries, and war booty, and supervised storage and distribution

function.zh: 组织皇帝近卫、召集地方军队、分配军役义务并统筹边防行动
function.en: Organized the imperial guard, mobilized provincial forces, assigned military obligations, and coordinated frontier operations

function.zh: 协调皇室与主教、修道院和教产关系，处理宗教捐赠与教义争端
function.en: Coordinated relations between the crown and bishops, monasteries, and church lands, and handled religious endowments and doctrinal disputes

function.zh: 传达皇帝裁决，依据习惯法、王令与宗教规范处理纠纷与惩处
function.en: Issued imperial judgments and handled disputes and punishments under customary law, royal commands, and religious norms

function.zh: 监督各省总督、边疆附庸与地方领主的忠诚、赋税与征兵责任
function.en: Oversaw the loyalty, taxation, and military obligations of provincial governors, frontier vassals, and local lords

function.zh: 管理王廷粮仓、驮畜运输、道路节点与军粮调度
function.en: Managed court granaries, pack-animal transport, road nodes, and military supply movements

localAdmin.zh: 地方治理依赖省级领主、世袭贵族、军政长官和教会地产管理者。中央对高原核心区控制较强，对南部、西部及东部边缘更多依赖贡赋、驻军和封臣关系。
localAdmin.en: Local rule depended on provincial lords, hereditary nobles, military-administrative commanders, and managers of church estates. Central control was stronger in the highland core, while the southern, western, and eastern fringes were governed more through tribute, garrisons, and vassal relations.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以基督教宗教规范、王室诏令、地方习惯法和司法仲裁相结合。1500年前后尚未形成后世成文法典的完整全国实施，司法很大程度取决于皇帝、贵族和教会法官的裁断。
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Christian religious norms, royal decrees, local customary law, and judicial arbitration. Around 1500 there was not yet a fully standardized empire-wide written code in later form; justice depended heavily on rulings by the emperor, nobles, and ecclesiastical judges.

taxationSystem.zh: 税收以土地与农产实物征收为主，包括谷物、牲畜、蜂蜜、布匹与劳役；边疆地区则多以贡赋形式纳税。战争战利品和附庸贡物也是重要财政来源。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation was based chiefly on land and agricultural production and was collected mostly in kind, including grain, livestock, honey, cloth, and labor service; frontier areas often paid in tribute. War booty and vassal offerings were also important sources of revenue.

religion.zh: 埃塞俄比亚正教基督教为国教，与修道传统、圣徒崇拜、斋戒制度和王权神圣观深度结合；同时帝国内亦存在穆斯林、犹太与地方信仰群体。
religion.en: Ethiopian Orthodox Christianity was the state religion and was deeply intertwined with monastic traditions, saint veneration, fasting practices, and sacred kingship; the empire also contained Muslim, Jewish, and local religious communities.

philosophy.zh: 思想核心为东方基督教神学、圣经诠释、修道主义、王权合法性观念与教会教育传统；知识活动多围绕宗教文本、礼仪、注释与辩论展开。
philosophy.en: Its intellectual core lay in Eastern Christian theology, biblical interpretation, monasticism, concepts of royal legitimacy, and ecclesiastical learning; scholarship focused largely on religious texts, liturgy, commentary, and debate.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为吉兹字母；吉兹语用于礼仪、经典与正式书写，阿姆哈拉语及其他地方语言用于口语和部分行政环境。
writingSystem.en: The principal writing system was the Ge'ez script; Ge'ez served for liturgy, scripture, and formal writing, while Amharic and other local languages were used in speech and in some administrative contexts.

culturalAchievements.zh: 以岩石教堂和高原教堂建筑、宗教手抄本彩绘、圣歌与礼仪音乐、王朝编年史、圣徒传记、十字架金属工艺及修道院教育网络著称。虽然15世纪晚期已过扎拉·雅各布时代的文化高峰，但帝国在1500年仍保持鲜明而独特的基督教高地文化。
culturalAchievements.en: It was distinguished by rock-hewn and highland church architecture, illuminated religious manuscripts, sacred chant and liturgical music, dynastic chronicles, hagiography, cross metalwork, and monastic educational networks. Although the cultural high point of Zara Yaqob’s era had passed by the late 15th century, in 1500 the empire still preserved a vivid and distinctive Christian highland culture.

languageFamily.zh: 以闪米特语族中的吉兹语、阿姆哈拉语、提格雷语和提格里尼亚语为核心，并包含库施特语族及其他地方语言
languageFamily.en: Centered on Semitic languages such as Ge'ez, Amharic, Tigray, and Tigrinya, while also including Cushitic and other regional languages

gdpDescription.zh: 帝国经济以高原农业和牧业为基础，整体生产能力在东北非属强国水平，但货币化程度较低，市场整合有限，许多财富以土地、牲畜和实物形式存在。按前现代比较尺度估计，其总量在撒哈拉以南非洲属较大经济体，但远低于欧亚大型帝国。
gdpDescription.en: The imperial economy rested on highland agriculture and pastoralism. Overall productive capacity was strong by northeast African standards, but monetization was limited and market integration remained modest, with much wealth held in land, livestock, and goods in kind. By premodern comparative standards it was one of the larger economies in sub-Saharan Africa, though far below the great empires of Eurasia.

mainIndustries.zh: 谷物种植、大麦与小麦农业、苔麸种植、畜牧、蜂蜜与蜂蜡生产、纺织、皮革、金属工艺、教会手抄本制作及地区性贸易
mainIndustries.en: Grain cultivation, barley and wheat farming, teff production, animal husbandry, honey and beeswax production, textiles, leatherworking, metal crafts, ecclesiastical manuscript production, and regional trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口或转运品包括黄金、象牙、奴隶、蜂蜡、麝香、皮革、咖啡原生区域产物与牲畜；输入品包括棉布、丝绸、金属器、盐、珠饰、香料和部分奢侈品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports or transit goods included gold, ivory, slaves, beeswax, civet, hides, products from early coffee-growing regions, and livestock; imports included cotton cloth, silk, metalwares, salt, beads, spices, and select luxury goods.

name.zh: 玛赫拉克与阿拉伯金银币并用

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富主要表现为耕地使用权、牲畜、谷物储备、蜂箱和家庭劳力。高地自由农民拥有一定稳定性，但遇战乱、旱灾或苛敛时易陷入贫困。贵族和教会则通过土地、贡赋和附庸劳动积累较大财富。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth was mainly embodied in cultivation rights, livestock, grain stores, beehives, and family labor. Highland free peasants had some stability, but could easily fall into hardship during war, drought, or heavy exactions. Nobles and the Church accumulated much larger wealth through land, tribute, and dependent labor.

economicSystem.zh: 以土地、贡赋和劳役为基础的农业帝国经济，辅以区域商路和红海联系；市场存在但不足以主导全帝国资源配置。
economicSystem.en: An agrarian imperial economy based on land, tribute, and labor service, supplemented by regional trade routes and Red Sea connections; markets existed but did not dominate resource allocation across the empire.

source.zh: 土地税与农业实物折值
source.en: Land tax and agricultural produce in equivalent value

source.zh: 战利品、罚没与非常征收

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非完全以铸币或贵金属保存，而是由金银器、外来货币、贡赋库存、粮仓、牲畜和可重新分配的王室物资共同构成。对于一座巡行帝国而言，此类分散式储备较为典型。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not held entirely in coin or bullion, but consisted of precious objects, foreign currency, tribute stocks, granaries, livestock, and redistributable royal supplies. Such a dispersed reserve was typical for an itinerant empire.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏向维持皇权、军队与贵族忠诚，依靠实物征收和临时加派而非稳定货币税制。稳定时期可积累余裕，但一旦发生远征、饥荒或边疆叛乱，财政弹性有限。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was oriented toward sustaining royal authority, the army, and noble loyalty, relying on in-kind collection and ad hoc levies rather than a stable monetized tax system. Surpluses could be accumulated in calm periods, but campaigns, famine, or frontier revolt quickly strained finances.

description.zh: 直属皇帝的核心兵力，负责保卫王家营地、执行突击任务并体现皇权威望。
description.en: Core forces directly attached to the emperor, guarding the royal camp, executing shock missions, and projecting imperial authority.

description.zh: 由各省贵族和军政长官依军役义务征集，是帝国战争中的主力，但训练和装备水平不一。
description.en: Raised by provincial nobles and military governors under service obligations; they formed the main wartime strength of the empire, though their training and equipment varied.

description.zh: 部署在东部和南部边境，适于侦察、袭扰、追击与应对机动性强的邻近穆斯林势力。
description.en: Stationed on the eastern and southern frontiers, suited for scouting, raiding, pursuit, and responding to mobile neighboring Muslim powers.

description.zh: 以传统弓矛兵为主，少量接触和使用火绳枪及外来火器，尚未成为决定性兵种。
description.en: Composed mainly of traditional bow- and spear-armed troops, with limited exposure to and use of matchlocks and imported firearms, not yet a decisive arm.

commanderInChief.zh: 皇帝纳奥德
commanderInChief.en: Emperor Na'od

technology.zh: 军备以长矛、剑、盾、弓箭和部分骑兵装备为主，冶铁与冷兵器传统成熟。火器知识已通过红海和穆斯林邻国传播，但在1500年尚处有限采用阶段，攻城与后勤能力主要依赖传统方式。
technology.en: Armament centered on spears, swords, shields, bows, and some cavalry equipment, supported by a mature ironworking and melee tradition. Knowledge of firearms had spread through the Red Sea and Muslim neighbors, but in 1500 adoption remained limited; siegecraft and logistics were still predominantly traditional.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自东部与东南部穆斯林政权和部族，尤其是阿达尔地区势力、索马里—阿法尔方向的边境冲突，以及边疆附庸的不稳定。对红海通道的控制不足也限制了军事物资获取。
threats.en: The main threats came from Muslim states and tribes to the east and southeast, especially powers in the Adal region, frontier conflict toward the Somali-Afar zone, and instability among peripheral vassals. Weak control over Red Sea access also limited the acquisition of military supplies.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要集中于埃塞俄比亚高原农业区，包括阿姆哈拉、提格雷、舍瓦等核心地带。边缘地区分布较疏，族群、语言和宗教差异显著。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated chiefly in the agricultural zones of the Ethiopian highlands, including the core regions of Amhara, Tigray, and Shewa. Peripheral regions were more sparsely settled and marked by major ethnic, linguistic, and religious diversity.

name.zh: 德布雷比尔汉周边宗教中心

socialClasses.zh: 皇室与大贵族、地方领主与军政贵族、高级教士与修士、自由农民、佃农与依附民、工匠商人、奴仆与战俘构成主要社会阶层。
socialClasses.en: The main social strata were the royal house and great nobles, provincial lords and military aristocracy, senior clergy and monks, free peasants, tenants and dependents, artisans and merchants, and servile people and captives.

allies.zh: 无稳定意义上的正式强国同盟，但与部分努比亚—上尼罗方向基督教或非穆斯林势力、红海贸易中介及寻求接触的葡萄牙人保持潜在合作空间。
allies.en: There were no stable great-power alliances in a formal sense, but the empire had potential for cooperation with some Christian or non-Muslim forces toward Nubia and the Upper Nile, Red Sea intermediaries, and the Portuguese who were seeking contact.

enemies.zh: 阿达尔苏丹国及其相关穆斯林边疆势力、部分索马里与阿法尔部族联盟，以及时常叛服不定的边缘附庸。
enemies.en: The Adal Sultanate and related Muslim frontier powers, some Somali and Afar tribal coalitions, and peripheral vassals of uncertain loyalty.

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以维护高原核心区安全、压制东部边疆威胁、争取红海贸易接触并寻求与其他基督教政权建立象征性联系为主。帝国重视宗教身份带来的合法性，但受地理封闭与通道受控限制，对远距离外交的执行能力有限。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on securing the highland core, containing eastern frontier threats, gaining access to Red Sea trade, and seeking symbolic ties with other Christian polities. The empire valued the legitimacy conferred by its Christian identity, but geographic isolation and constrained trade routes limited its capacity for long-distance diplomacy.

keyInnovations.zh: 技术基础以高原农耕、梯田与犁耕、石木建筑、冶铁、手抄本制作和教会教育为核心。帝国已知晓并少量接触火器、红海航运物资与更广泛的旧大陆交流成果，但总体技术扩散速度慢于地中海和印度洋核心区。
keyInnovations.en: Its technological base rested on highland farming, terracing and plow cultivation, stone-and-timber construction, ironworking, manuscript production, and ecclesiastical education. The empire had become aware of and made limited use of firearms, Red Sea maritime imports, and wider Old World exchanges, but overall diffusion was slower than in the Mediterranean and Indian Ocean core zones.

strengths.zh: 拥有强烈的王朝合法性与宗教凝聚力，高原地形利于防御，农业基础稳固，贵族—教会—王权结构能够在稳定时期维持帝国统合。作为东北非主要基督教国家，其身份在外交上具有独特价值。
strengths.en: It possessed strong dynastic legitimacy and religious cohesion, a defensible highland geography, a solid agrarian base, and a noble-church-crown structure capable of sustaining imperial integration in stable times. As the leading Christian state of northeast Africa, its identity carried distinctive diplomatic value.

weaknesses.zh: 中央集权有限，财政和军队高度依赖地方贵族，边疆控制薄弱，外海通道受制于他者，火器与攻城技术相对落后。王位继承的不确定性也可能引发内斗。
weaknesses.en: Centralization was limited, finances and military power depended heavily on provincial nobles, frontier control was weak, access to maritime routes was constrained by others, and firearms and siege technology lagged behind. Uncertain succession could also trigger internal conflict.

outlook.zh: 1500年的埃塞俄比亚帝国总体稳定，仍是东北非重要强权，但其未来将取决于能否应对东部穆斯林军事压力、改善火器获取、稳住贵族联盟并维持王朝权威。若外部威胁加剧而内部整合不足，帝国将面临严峻考验。
outlook.en: In 1500 the Ethiopian Empire was broadly stable and remained a major power in northeast Africa, but its future depended on its ability to withstand eastern Muslim military pressure, improve access to firearms, preserve noble alliances, and maintain dynastic authority. If external threats intensified while internal integration remained weak, the empire would face severe tests.

description.zh: 埃塞俄比亚帝国是东北非最重要的基督教国家，保持高原核心统治。
description.en: The Ethiopian Empire was the leading Christian state of northeast Africa, ruling its highland core.

name.zh: 索马里-阿法尔伊斯兰文明

ruler.zh: 穆罕默德·本·艾资哈尔·阿德-丁

governmentForm.zh: 以穆斯林王朝为核心的苏丹制国家，结合宫廷、宗教学者、部族首领与商贸城市精英共同治理
governmentForm.en: A Muslim dynastic sultanate centered on the court, governed through a combination of palace authority, religious scholars, clan leaders, and mercantile urban elites

socialStructure.zh: 社会由苏丹与王室、军事贵族、宗教学者、商人、牧民、农民、手工业者与奴隶人口构成；城市与商路节点较为多元，乡村和草原则以氏族与部族网络为主
socialStructure.en: Society consisted of the sultan and royal house, military nobles, religious scholars, merchants, pastoralists, cultivators, artisans, and enslaved people; urban trade centers were relatively diverse, while rural and steppe zones were organized mainly through clan and tribal networks

rulingClass.zh: 王室、军事酋长、阿拉伯化与本地化穆斯林法学者、税务与港市官员，以及与宫廷结盟的氏族领袖
rulingClass.en: The ruling class included the royal family, military chiefs, Arabized and local Muslim jurists, fiscal and port officials, and clan leaders allied to the court

succession.zh: 王朝世袭为原则，但通常须得到宫廷显贵、军政首领与宗教精英承认，继承实践中常伴随派系协商
succession.en: Succession was formally dynastic and hereditary, but in practice required recognition from court grandees, military leaders, and religious elites, often involving factional negotiation

structure.zh: 中央政权围绕苏丹宫廷运作，下设财政、军务、宗教司法、商路与城镇管理机构；地方上依赖总督、城镇长官及部族附庸体系维持控制
structure.en: The central government operated around the sultan’s court, with institutions for finance, military affairs, religious justice, trade-route oversight, and town administration; locally it relied on governors, urban officers, and tributary tribal structures

function.zh: 起草敕令、管理印玺、协调朝廷文书与使节往来
function.en: Drafted decrees, managed the seal, and coordinated court records and diplomatic correspondence

function.zh: 征收地税、商税、关税、贡赋与港口收费，管理国库与军饷
function.en: Collected land taxes, commercial levies, customs, tribute, and port dues, and managed the treasury and army pay

function.zh: 调度常备军、征召部族兵、供应武器牲畜并维护边境防御
function.en: Directed the standing forces, mobilized tribal levies, supplied arms and animals, and maintained frontier defense

function.zh: 管理伊斯兰司法、清真寺与宗教捐赠，监督教育与法学事务
function.en: Oversaw Islamic justice, mosques, and religious endowments, and supervised education and legal scholarship

name.en: Trade Routes and Urban Administration Office

function.zh: 维护市场秩序、商队驿路、水源与仓储，监管主要城镇和贸易据点
function.en: Maintained market order, caravan routes, water points, and storage facilities, and supervised major towns and trade stations

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政由核心城镇总督、边区军事长官、卡迪和部族首领共同构成。苏丹国在密集贸易区与定居区控制较强，在游牧腹地则更多依靠盟约、纳贡和军事威慑维系统治。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was composed of governors in core towns, frontier military commanders, qadis, and clan leaders. The sultanate exercised stronger control in dense trade and settled zones, while in pastoral hinterlands it ruled more through alliance, tribute, and military deterrence.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以伊斯兰教法中的沙斐仪传统为主，并结合苏丹敕令、地方习惯法和部族调解机制；商贸纠纷多由卡迪审理，部族冲突常通过赔偿与盟约处理。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was based primarily on Shafi‘i Islamic law, supplemented by sultanic decrees, local custom, and tribal mediation; commercial disputes were commonly heard by qadis, while clan conflicts were often settled through compensation and negotiated accords.

taxationSystem.zh: 税制包括农业地租、牲畜税、市场税、商队通行税、港口及关卡税和附庸贡赋。对海陆贸易征税是财政的重要来源，实物征收与银币、金银称量并行。
taxationSystem.en: Taxation included agricultural rents, livestock taxes, market dues, caravan transit tolls, port and customs duties, and tribute from subordinate groups. Taxing overland and maritime trade was a major fiscal pillar, with collection made in kind as well as in coin and weighed precious metals.

religion.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教为主，属沙斐仪学派；苏菲实践、圣裔声望与地方宗教传统并存，在城市和贸易网络中影响尤强
religion.en: Sunni Islam of the Shafi‘i school predominated; Sufi practice, sharifian prestige, and local religious traditions coexisted, with especially strong influence in towns and trade networks

philosophy.zh: 思想以伊斯兰法学、伦理学、经注传统和政治忠诚观为核心，同时重视部族荣誉、盟约义务与口述历史
philosophy.en: Intellectual life centered on Islamic jurisprudence, ethics, exegetical traditions, and ideas of political loyalty, while also valuing clan honor, obligations of alliance, and oral historical memory

writingSystem.zh: 官方与宗教学术主要使用阿拉伯文；地方语言多以口传为主，但存在阿拉伯文字记录、商业记述与宗教文本书写
writingSystem.en: Arabic was used for official and religious scholarship; local languages were largely oral, though Arabic script was used for records, commercial notation, and religious texts

culturalAchievements.zh: 文化成就包括清真寺与石构宗教建筑、阿拉伯文法学与书信传统、跨红海贸易文化、马术与战争诗歌、以及索马里和阿法尔口述史诗与家族谱系记忆。
culturalAchievements.en: Cultural achievements included mosques and stone religious architecture, Arabic legal and epistolary traditions, a Red Sea-oriented trading culture, equestrian and martial poetry, and Somali and Afar oral epics and genealogical memory.

languageFamily.zh: 主要语言属亚非语系库希特语支，行政与宗教精英广泛使用阿拉伯语
languageFamily.en: The main vernaculars belonged to the Cushitic branch of the Afroasiatic family, while Arabic was widely used among administrative and religious elites

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以牧业、区域农业和转口贸易结合为特征。按1500年前后红海—亚丁湾体系标准衡量，阿达尔的总体产出在非洲之角属较强水平，但人均财富受生态脆弱性与战争风险限制。
gdpDescription.en: The economy combined pastoral production, regional agriculture, and transit trade. By the standards of the Red Sea–Gulf of Aden system around 1500, Adal’s total output was strong for the Horn of Africa, though per capita wealth was constrained by ecological fragility and the risk of war.

mainIndustries.zh: 畜牧业、谷物种植、乳制品、皮革加工、盐与乳香等区域产品交换、商队运输、港市贸易、纺织品转售与武器马匹输入
mainIndustries.en: Pastoralism, grain cultivation, dairy production, leatherworking, exchange in regional goods such as salt and incense, caravan transport, port commerce, resale of textiles, and importation of weapons and horses

tradeGoods.zh: 出口牲畜、皮张、乳香、没药、奴隶、象牙及区域农产品；进口棉布、丝绸、珠饰、金属器、刀剑、火器少量、马匹、香料与奢侈品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included livestock, hides, frankincense, myrrh, enslaved people, ivory, and regional agricultural produce; imports included cotton cloth, silk, beads, metalware, swords, limited firearms, horses, spices, and luxury goods

name.zh: 银币与称量贵金属并用
name.en: Mixed silver coinage and weighed bullion

householdWealth.zh: 家庭财富差异明显。牧民财富多以骆驼、羊群和盟友网络体现；城市商人财富集中于库存、债权、房产与进口消费品；农户财富较低且受旱灾影响显著。
householdWealth.en: Household wealth varied sharply. Pastoral wealth was measured largely in camels, flocks, and alliance networks; urban merchant wealth was concentrated in inventories, credit, property, and imported consumer goods; peasant households were poorer and highly vulnerable to drought.

economicSystem.zh: 以宫廷抽税、部族牧业、市场交换和跨区域商贸相结合的前近代混合经济。国家直接控制有限，但通过税关、盟约和军事保护对流通环节影响显著。
economicSystem.en: A premodern mixed economy combining court taxation, tribal pastoral production, market exchange, and long-distance commerce. Direct state control was limited, but the state strongly influenced circulation through customs, alliances, and military protection.

source.en: Commercial taxes, customs, and port dues

source.zh: 附庸纳贡、罚没与杂项收入

category.zh: 宗教机构、法学教育与捐赠
category.en: Religious institutions, legal learning, and endowments

category.zh: 道路、水源、仓储与市场维护
category.en: Roads, water points, storage, and market maintenance

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库储备中等偏稳健，以银币、称量银、进口奢侈品和可动用牲畜资源构成。稳定时期足以维持宫廷和边防，但若与埃塞俄比亚长期全面战争，财政压力会迅速上升。
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was moderately sound, consisting of silver coin, weighed silver, imported luxury goods, and mobilizable livestock resources. In stable times it was sufficient for court and frontier needs, but a prolonged full-scale war with Ethiopia would strain finances rapidly.

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策倾向稳健保守，重视维持商路安全与边防供给，避免过度压榨核心商人和部族盟友；在危机时期则通过临时贡赋、没收和战时征发补充收入。
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy was generally cautious and conservative, prioritizing secure trade routes and frontier provisioning while avoiding excessive pressure on core merchants and allied clans; in crisis, revenue was supplemented by emergency tribute, confiscation, and wartime requisition.

description.zh: 由宫廷直接供养，负责首都防卫、要塞驻守和战时核心步兵作战，装备矛、盾、弓箭及部分输入火器
description.en: Maintained directly by the court, this force defended the capital, garrisoned forts, and formed the core wartime infantry, equipped with spears, shields, bows, and a limited number of imported firearms

description.zh: 用于快速突袭、边境巡逻和追击，依托草原与半干旱地形机动优势，是阿达尔军队的关键精锐
description.en: Used for rapid raids, border patrols, and pursuit, these troops exploited steppe and semi-arid mobility and formed a key elite element of Adal’s army

description.zh: 由附庸氏族与盟友提供，战时大规模集结，擅长区域机动作战、伏击和补给支援，但纪律与持久作战能力较不稳定
description.en: Supplied by subordinate clans and allies, these forces were mobilized in larger numbers in war and excelled in regional mobility, ambush, and logistical support, though discipline and sustained campaign capacity were less reliable

description.zh: 驻守主要市镇、税关和商路水源点，保护商队、仓储与财政据点
description.en: Stationed in principal towns, customs posts, and water points on trade routes, they protected caravans, storage depots, and fiscal centers

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹穆罕默德·本·艾资哈尔·阿德-丁
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Muhammad ibn Azhar ad-Din

technology.zh: 军备以传统矛盾、弓箭、刀剑、皮盾和骑乘机动为主，已接触并有限使用来自红海和阿拉伯贸易网络输入的火器与火药技术，但尚未形成大规模标准化火器军队。
technology.en: Armament relied primarily on traditional spears, bows, swords, leather shields, and mounted mobility, with some access to firearms and gunpowder technology imported through Red Sea and Arabian trade networks, though not yet on a large standardized scale.

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自埃塞俄比亚基督教政权的边境冲突与宗教政治竞争，其次是内部部族离心、旱灾引发的补给危机，以及红海贸易格局变化带来的军资压力。
threats.en: The primary threat came from frontier conflict and politico-religious rivalry with the Ethiopian Christian state, followed by internal clan centrifugal tendencies, supply crises caused by drought, and military-financial pressure from changes in Red Sea trade patterns.

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要分布于贸易节点、绿洲化定居地、农业区和广阔牧场地带。整体密度不高，但商路沿线与核心城镇人口集聚明显，人口结构兼具游牧、半定居和城市商业特征。
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated in trade nodes, oasis-like settled areas, agricultural districts, and broad pastoral lands. Overall density was low, but populations clustered strongly along caravan routes and in core towns, with a demographic structure combining nomadic, semi-settled, and urban mercantile elements.

socialClasses.zh: 上层为苏丹王室、军政贵族、宗教学者与大商人；中层为中小商人、城镇手工业者、地方头人和富裕牧民；下层为普通牧民、农民、劳役者与奴隶人口。社会流动主要依靠军事功绩、宗教学识、婚姻联盟和商业积累。
socialClasses.en: The upper strata consisted of the sultan’s family, military-administrative nobility, religious scholars, and major merchants; the middle strata included lesser merchants, urban artisans, local headmen, and prosperous pastoralists; the lower strata comprised ordinary herders, cultivators, laborers, and enslaved people. Social mobility came chiefly through military service, religious learning, marriage alliances, and commercial accumulation.

allies.zh: 主要依赖阿拉伯半岛与红海沿岸的穆斯林商人与宗教联系网络，并与部分斯瓦希里海岸与地区穆斯林政权保持友好贸易往来
allies.en: It relied chiefly on Muslim mercantile and religious networks along Arabia and the Red Sea, while maintaining friendly trade relations with parts of the Swahili Coast and regional Muslim polities

enemies.zh: 最主要的敌手是埃塞俄比亚基督教文明；边疆上也常与不稳定附庸、竞争性部族和争夺商路的地方势力发生冲突
enemies.en: Its principal adversary was the Ethiopian Christian civilization; on the frontiers it also frequently clashed with unstable tributaries, rival clans, and local powers competing for trade routes

foreignPolicy.zh: 外交政策以控制内陆—海岸商路、巩固伊斯兰共同体联系、牵制埃塞俄比亚扩张并争取外来军资为核心。对远方大国多通过贸易间接接触，而非正式对等外交。
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling inland-to-coastal trade routes, reinforcing ties within the Islamic ecumene, checking Ethiopian expansion, and securing external military supplies. Contact with distant great powers was mostly indirect through trade rather than formal peer diplomacy.

keyInnovations.zh: 关键技术与能力包括干旱地带商队组织、骆驼与骑兵机动体系、红海航贸网络中的导航与账簿实践、石构清真寺建筑、蓄水与驿路节点管理，以及有限吸收火药武器。
keyInnovations.en: Key technologies and capabilities included caravan organization in arid lands, camel and cavalry mobility systems, navigational and bookkeeping practices tied to Red Sea commerce, stone mosque construction, water-storage and route-node management, and limited adoption of gunpowder weapons.

strengths.zh: 该文明的优势在于连接非洲之角内陆与红海世界的地理位置、强韧的牧业基础、灵活的部族军事动员、伊斯兰商贸网络支撑，以及对埃塞俄比亚形成持续战略压力的能力。
strengths.en: Its strengths lay in its geographic position linking the Horn interior to the Red Sea world, a resilient pastoral base, flexible clan-based military mobilization, support from Islamic commercial networks, and the capacity to exert sustained strategic pressure on Ethiopia.

weaknesses.zh: 弱点在于中央集权有限、财政高度依赖贸易与贡赋、生态环境易受旱灾冲击、地方氏族忠诚度波动，以及火器与攻城技术仍相对有限。
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses included limited centralization, a fiscal system highly dependent on trade and tribute, ecological vulnerability to drought, fluctuating clan loyalty, and still-limited firearms and siege capabilities.

outlook.zh: 至1500年其局势总体稳定且具扩张潜力。若能进一步整合宗教号召、贸易财富和外来火器来源，未来数十年有可能在对埃塞俄比亚战争中取得更大突破；但若商路受扰、王朝继承不稳或气候恶化，则稳定性将迅速下降。
outlook.en: By 1500 its condition was broadly stable and expansionary in potential. If it could further integrate religious mobilization, commercial wealth, and access to imported firearms, it might achieve greater breakthroughs in war against Ethiopia in the coming decades; however, disruption of trade routes, dynastic instability, or climatic deterioration could quickly undermine that stability.

description.zh: 阿达尔控制非洲之角部分内陆与贸易通道，是埃塞俄比亚的重要对手。
description.en: Adal controlled parts of the Horn's interior and trade routes and was a major rival of Ethiopia.

name.zh: 上尼罗河苏丹文明（丰吉苏丹国）

governmentForm.zh: 以苏丹为核心的尼罗河谷伊斯兰君主制，结合部族联盟、宫廷亲族政治与地方附庸统治
governmentForm.en: A Nile Valley Islamic monarchy centered on the sultan, combining tribal confederation, court kin politics, and local tributary rule

socialStructure.zh: 社会由苏丹与宫廷贵族、军事随从、宗教学者与法官、阿拉伯化与努比亚化地方首领、城镇商人、农民、牧民、手工业者及奴隶组成，族群与身份关系仍具流动性
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of the sultan and court nobility, military retainers, religious scholars and judges, Arabized and Nubianized local chiefs, urban merchants, peasants, pastoralists, artisans, and slaves, with ethnicity and status remaining relatively fluid

rulingClass.zh: 丰吉王族、宫廷军政贵族、与苏丹结盟的部族酋长及新兴伊斯兰宗教精英
rulingClass.en: The Funj royal lineage, court military-administrative nobles, allied tribal chiefs, and an emerging Islamic religious elite

succession.zh: 王位主要在丰吉王族内部继承，通常由强势成年男性经宫廷、军队与显贵支持而确认，带有宗族协商和武力竞争色彩
succession.en: The throne was inherited within the Funj royal house, usually confirmed for a strong adult male through court, military, and elite support, with elements of clan negotiation and armed competition

structure.zh: 国家处于扩张与整合初期，中央朝廷设于森纳尔，由苏丹统辖宫廷、军队、贡赋网络与对外关系；地方则依赖河谷据点、附庸首领、税吏和宗教人士维系秩序
structure.en: The state was in an early phase of expansion and consolidation, with the central court at Sennar controlling the palace, army, tribute networks, and foreign relations, while the provinces relied on riverine strongholds, tributary chiefs, tax collectors, and religious figures to maintain order

function.zh: 管理王室财产、宫廷礼仪、侍从、王族封地与接见事务
function.en: Managed royal property, court ceremony, attendants, royal estates, and formal audiences

function.zh: 征收谷物、牲畜、黄金、奴隶、关税及地方贡品，并记录分配
function.en: Collected grain, livestock, gold, slaves, customs dues, and provincial tribute, and recorded their distribution

function.zh: 组织苏丹亲军、调集附庸兵力、守卫河道与边界并处理对努比亚与草原地区的征讨
function.en: Organized the sultan's household troops, mobilized tributary forces, guarded river routes and frontiers, and handled campaigns toward Nubia and steppe regions

function.zh: 支持卡迪审判、清真寺与学者网络，推动伊斯兰法与地方习惯的协调
function.en: Supported qadi courts, mosques, and scholarly networks, and coordinated Islamic law with local custom

function.zh: 监管市场、河港、渡口和长途商队税收，维护森纳尔的商业地位
function.en: Supervised markets, river ports, ferries, and caravan taxation, preserving Sennar's commercial role

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政并非高度官僚化，多由受封首领、军事总管、河谷聚落长老与宗教人士共同治理；尼罗河沿岸地区控制较强，外围草原与游牧地带则通过朝贡、婚盟与武力威慑维持
localAdmin.en: Local administration was not highly bureaucratized and was often shared by appointed chiefs, military overseers, village elders, and religious figures; control was stronger along the Nile corridor, while outer savanna and pastoral zones were held through tribute, marriage alliances, and coercive force

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系为伊斯兰法、苏丹敕令与地方习惯法并存；婚姻、继承和商事日益受沙里亚影响，但土地、牧场、部族赔偿与身份关系仍大量依赖习惯法裁断
legalSystem.en: The legal system combined Islamic law, sultanic decrees, and customary law; marriage, inheritance, and commerce were increasingly shaped by sharia, but land, pasture, tribal compensation, and status relations were still judged largely through custom

taxationSystem.zh: 财政基础主要来自谷物实物税、牲畜贡赋、黄金与奴隶贡品、河运和商道关税、市场税以及附庸首领定期进贡
taxationSystem.en: The fiscal base came chiefly from grain taxes in kind, livestock tribute, gold and slave tribute, riverine and caravan customs, market dues, and regular payments from subordinate chiefs

religion.zh: 伊斯兰教逊尼派正在成为国家与精英层主导信仰，但民间仍保留大量努比亚、苏丹草原与部族传统信仰实践，宗教转化处于进行中
religion.en: Sunni Islam was becoming the dominant religion of the state and elite, but popular life still retained many Nubian, Sudanese savanna, and tribal religious practices, making Islamization an ongoing process

philosophy.zh: 思想世界以伊斯兰政治合法性、苏非虔敬、部族荣誉伦理与尼罗河谷传统权威观念相结合，重视统治者的武力、慷慨与宗教庇护
philosophy.en: Its intellectual world combined Islamic political legitimacy, Sufi piety, tribal honor ethics, and older Nile Valley concepts of authority, emphasizing the ruler's force, generosity, and religious patronage

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写体系为阿拉伯字母，用于宗教、外交、贸易与法律记录；地方口语多未标准化书写，口传传统仍极其重要
writingSystem.en: The main writing system was the Arabic script, used for religion, diplomacy, trade, and legal records; most local vernaculars were not standardized in writing, and oral tradition remained extremely important

culturalAchievements.zh: 森纳尔作为新兴都城，推动了尼罗河上游伊斯兰宫廷文化的形成；清真寺、宗教教育、阿拉伯文文书、长诗与家谱传统逐渐发展，同时吸收了努比亚与苏丹草原的服饰、音乐和仪礼元素
culturalAchievements.en: As an emerging capital, Sennar fostered the formation of an Upper Nile Islamic court culture; mosques, religious learning, Arabic documentary practice, long-form poetry, and genealogical traditions gradually developed while incorporating Nubian and Sudanese savanna elements in dress, music, and ceremony

languageFamily.zh: 语言环境多元，包括阿拉伯语、努比亚语支语言及苏丹地区尼罗-撒哈拉和其他地方语言；阿拉伯语是宗教和跨区域交流语言
languageFamily.en: The linguistic environment was diverse, including Arabic, Nubian languages, and Nilo-Saharan and other local Sudanese tongues; Arabic served as the language of religion and wider regional communication

gdpDescription.zh: 经济以尼罗河谷农业、牲畜放牧、区域转运贸易和贡赋掠取为主，整体财富水平不及埃及、奥斯曼或印度大国，但在东北非内陆已属重要新兴政权
gdpDescription.en: The economy rested on Nile Valley agriculture, livestock herding, regional transit trade, and tribute extraction; overall wealth was below that of Egypt, the Ottomans, or the great Indian polities, but it was an important rising power in interior northeast Africa

mainIndustries.zh: 高粱与谷物种植、牲畜饲养、尼罗河渔业、皮革与纺织、地方金矿采集、盐与铁器交换、奴隶贸易、河运与商队贸易
mainIndustries.en: Sorghum and grain cultivation, livestock raising, Nile fishing, leatherworking and textiles, local gold extraction, salt and iron exchange, slave trading, and riverine and caravan commerce

tradeGoods.zh: 出口奴隶、黄金、象牙、牲畜、皮革、阿拉伯胶类产品与部分谷物；输入纺织品、金属器、马匹、珠饰、纸张、奢侈品与伊斯兰学术用品
tradeGoods.en: Exports included slaves, gold, ivory, livestock, leather, gum products, and some grain; imports included textiles, metal goods, horses, beads, paper, luxury wares, and Islamic scholarly materials

name.zh: 混合实物与金属计值体系
name.en: Mixed commodity and metallic accounting system

householdWealth.zh: 普通家庭财富多以谷仓存粮、牛羊、生产工具与亲属网络体现，而非大量铸币储蓄；城市商人与宫廷贵族拥有更高流动财富
householdWealth.en: Ordinary household wealth was expressed mainly through stored grain, cattle and sheep, tools, and kin networks rather than large coin savings; urban merchants and court nobles possessed greater liquid wealth

economicSystem.zh: 经济制度为农业贡赋、部族再分配与市场交换并行的早期苏丹国体制，国家对关键商道、河渡和贡品流通具有较强干预
economicSystem.en: The economic system was an early sultanate order combining agrarian tribute, tribal redistribution, and market exchange, with the state exerting strong influence over key trade routes, river crossings, and tribute flows

source.zh: 农业实物税与河谷地租
source.en: Agrarian taxes in kind and riverine rents

source.zh: 黄金、奴隶与战利品收入

category.en: Court, royal household, and noble patronage

treasuryDescription.zh: 国库并非全部为硬币，而是由金属、贡品、牲畜、粮食储备和可分配奢侈品共同构成；其稳健程度依赖尼罗河收成、地方忠诚与贸易通路安全
treasuryDescription.en: The treasury was not held entirely in coin but consisted of metal reserves, tribute goods, livestock, grain stocks, and distributable luxury items; its stability depended on Nile harvests, provincial loyalty, and the security of trade routes

fiscalPolicy.zh: 财政政策偏重维持军事动员、赏赐网络和政治整合，征收方式以实物与贡赋为主，中央倾向在丰年积储、在扩张或平叛时期加重动员
fiscalPolicy.en: Fiscal policy prioritized military mobilization, patronage networks, and political integration, with revenue collected mainly in kind and tribute; the center tended to store surpluses in good years and intensify extraction during expansion or rebellion

description.zh: 驻守森纳尔及核心河谷据点，装备长矛、盾牌、弓箭和少量刀剑，是维系王权的主要常备力量
description.en: Stationed in Sennar and core riverine strongholds, armed with spears, shields, bows, and some swords, forming the main standing force sustaining royal authority

description.zh: 数量有限但机动性强，多由贵族和盟友提供，用于追击、侦察、边境威慑与快速掠袭
description.en: Limited in number but mobile, supplied largely by nobles and allies, and used for pursuit, reconnaissance, frontier intimidation, and rapid raiding

description.zh: 由地方首领按需征集，战时构成主力，作战方式受部族传统影响，适合河谷防御与草原机动战
description.en: Raised by local chiefs as needed, these formed the main wartime mass and fought in ways shaped by tribal tradition, suitable for river defense and savanna maneuver warfare

name.en: River Patrol and Ferry Garrison Forces

description.zh: 负责守卫蓝尼罗河交通、征税点和关键渡口，对商贸与行政控制尤为重要
description.en: Tasked with guarding Blue Nile traffic, tax points, and key ferry crossings, they were especially important to commercial and administrative control

commanderInChief.zh: 苏丹阿玛拉·敦卡斯
commanderInChief.en: Sultan Amara Dunqas

technology.zh: 军事技术以长矛、弓箭、盾牌、刀剑和骑乘作战为主，火器若有也极少且未制度化；后勤依赖河运、牲畜与地方征发
technology.en: Military technology was centered on spears, bows, shields, blades, and mounted warfare; firearms, if present at all, were extremely rare and not institutionalized; logistics depended on river transport, livestock, and local requisitioning

threats.zh: 主要威胁来自北方残余努比亚势力、与埃及—红海贸易体系相关的外部压力、南部和西部边缘地区的不稳定部族，以及对新征服地区忠诚度不足
threats.en: Main threats came from residual Nubian forces to the north, external pressures tied to the Egypt-Red Sea trade sphere, unstable tribes on the southern and western margins, and uncertain loyalty in newly subdued territories

populationDescription.zh: 人口主要沿蓝尼罗河、白尼罗河汇流地带及其支流分布，河谷聚居明显，草原和半干旱地带则以分散牧业人口为主
populationDescription.en: The population was concentrated mainly along the Blue Nile, the confluence zone with the White Nile, and nearby tributaries, with clear riverine settlement patterns, while savanna and semi-arid areas were dominated by dispersed pastoral populations

socialClasses.zh: 上层为王族、军事贵族、部族酋长和宗教学者；中层为商人、书记、手工业者与较富裕农牧民；下层为普通农民、牧民、劳役人口与奴隶。社会边界存在流动，但政治权力高度集中于宫廷及其盟友
socialClasses.en: The upper ranks consisted of the royal house, military nobles, tribal chiefs, and religious scholars; the middle ranks included merchants, scribes, artisans, and wealthier agro-pastoral households; the lower ranks were ordinary peasants, pastoralists, laborers, and slaves. Social boundaries were somewhat fluid, but political power remained concentrated in the court and its allies

allies.zh: 主要盟友为臣属或合作性的阿拉伯化部族、部分河谷地方首领以及与森纳尔贸易互利的红海和内陆商人网络
allies.en: Its main allies were subordinate or cooperative Arabized tribes, certain local riverine chiefs, and Red Sea and inland merchant networks benefiting from trade with Sennar

enemies.zh: 主要敌对对象是衰落中的努比亚基督教残余政权、拒绝纳贡的边缘部族，以及在上游和边境地带争夺税源与通路的地方势力
enemies.en: Its chief enemies were the declining residual Nubian Christian polities, frontier tribes resisting tribute, and local forces competing for taxable routes and resources in upriver and border zones

foreignPolicy.zh: 对外政策以控制尼罗河上游贸易、吞并或附庸旧努比亚地区、稳固与红海及埃及方向的商业联系并防止边疆离心为核心
foreignPolicy.en: Foreign policy focused on controlling Upper Nile trade, annexing or subordinating former Nubian territories, stabilizing commercial links toward the Red Sea and Egypt, and preventing centrifugal tendencies on the frontiers

keyInnovations.zh: 关键能力不在机械突破，而在将阿拉伯文书、伊斯兰法学、河运组织、长途商队网络与苏丹草原军事动员方式结合起来，形成适应尼罗河—草原边界地带的国家体系
keyInnovations.en: Its key advances lay not in mechanical breakthroughs but in combining Arabic documentary practice, Islamic jurisprudence, river transport organization, long-distance caravan networks, and Sudanese savanna modes of military mobilization into a state system suited to the Nile-savanna frontier

strengths.zh: 国家最大的优势在于掌握蓝尼罗河核心走廊、具备较强军事动员能力、拥有上升中的伊斯兰合法性，并能连接埃及、红海与非洲内陆贸易
strengths.en: The state's chief strengths were control of the core Blue Nile corridor, a comparatively strong capacity for military mobilization, rising Islamic legitimacy, and the ability to connect Egypt, the Red Sea, and the African interior

weaknesses.zh: 其弱点在于行政制度尚未成熟、地方整合依赖个人忠诚与贡赋关系、财政实物化程度高、城市化有限且边疆控制时强时弱
weaknesses.en: Its weaknesses were an immature administrative system, provincial integration dependent on personal loyalty and tribute ties, a heavily in-kind fiscal structure, limited urbanization, and uneven frontier control

outlook.zh: 到1500年前后，丰吉苏丹国前景总体乐观。若能继续吸纳宗教精英、巩固河谷税基并压制地方离心势力，它有望成为东北非内陆长期主导政权；但若继承冲突加剧或贸易线路转移，国家稳定也可能迅速受损
outlook.en: Around 1500, the Funj Sultanate's outlook was broadly favorable. If it continued to incorporate religious elites, strengthen the riverine tax base, and suppress provincial separatism, it could become a long-lasting dominant power in interior northeast Africa; but intensified succession struggles or shifts in trade routes could quickly undermine its stability

description.zh: 丰吉政权在青尼罗河流域兴起，逐渐取代努比亚旧基督教王国。
description.en: The Funj state was rising along the Blue Nile, gradually replacing the older Nubian Christian kingdoms.

governmentForm.zh: 以海港城邦为基础、由基尔瓦苏丹宣称宗主权的伊斯兰商人君主制
governmentForm.en: An Islamic mercantile monarchy centered on port city-states, with the Sultan of Kilwa claiming overlordship over much of the coast

socialStructure.zh: 社会以沿海穆斯林城镇精英、商人家族、宗教学者、工匠、渔民、航海者、自由平民与奴隶构成；内陆非穆斯林社群通过贸易与海岸相连。
socialStructure.en: Society was composed of coastal Muslim urban elites, merchant lineages, religious scholars, artisans, fishermen, sailors, free commoners, and slaves; inland non-Muslim communities were linked to the coast through trade.

rulingClass.zh: 阿拉伯-非洲混合血统的斯瓦希里穆斯林商人贵族与王室家族
rulingClass.en: Swahili Muslim merchant aristocrats and royal families of mixed African and Arab heritage

succession.zh: 王位通常在统治家族内部世袭，但常受城中显贵、商人集团与宗教精英支持或争夺影响
succession.en: The throne was generally hereditary within the ruling family, but succession was often shaped or contested by urban notables, merchant factions, and religious elites

structure.zh: 政治结构较为分散。基尔瓦苏丹直接控制都城及若干附属港口，并通过地方总督、亲族、朝贡关系和商业依附影响其他斯瓦希里城邦，但对蒙巴萨、马林迪等地的控制在1500年前后已明显减弱。
structure.en: The political structure was decentralized. The Sultan of Kilwa directly controlled the capital and several dependent ports, and influenced other Swahili city-states through governors, kinship ties, tributary arrangements, and commercial dependence, but control over places such as Mombasa and Malindi had clearly weakened by around 1500.

function.zh: 征收港口关税、贡赋、市场税与王室地产收入，管理金银与货物储备
function.en: Collected customs duties, tribute, market taxes, and royal estate income, and managed reserves of gold, silver, and goods

function.zh: 监管港口泊位、航运秩序、度量衡、进出口查验与对外商接待
function.en: Supervised berths, maritime order, weights and measures, import-export inspection, and reception of foreign merchants

function.zh: 以阿拉伯文和斯瓦希里文记录诏令、契约、账册、外交信函与宗教捐赠文书
function.en: Recorded decrees, contracts, ledgers, diplomatic correspondence, and religious endowment documents in Arabic and Swahili

function.zh: 处理伊斯兰法事务、商业纠纷、婚姻继承与城市秩序案件
function.en: Handled matters of Islamic law, commercial disputes, marriage and inheritance, and cases concerning urban order

function.zh: 维持宫廷运作、保护苏丹、统领近卫与调度海岸防务
function.en: Maintained palace operations, protected the sultan, commanded the guard, and coordinated coastal defense

localAdmin.zh: 地方行政以各港口城镇自治为主，由谢赫、总督、港务官、税吏与卡迪管理。基尔瓦对南部诸港影响较强，但对远方城邦更多依赖结盟、联姻、贸易控制与象征性朝贡。
localAdmin.en: Local administration was primarily urban and port-based, managed by shaykhs, governors, harbor officials, tax collectors, and qadis. Kilwa exercised stronger influence over southern ports, but over distant city-states it relied more on alliance, marriage ties, trade control, and symbolic tribute.

legalSystem.zh: 法律体系以逊尼派伊斯兰法（主要为沙斐仪派传统）为核心，辅以地方习惯法和商人惯例，尤其重视债务、合伙、航运、奴隶买卖与继承问题。
legalSystem.en: The legal system was centered on Sunni Islamic law, mainly in the Shafi'i tradition, supplemented by local custom and merchant practice, with special concern for debt, partnership, shipping, slave sales, and inheritance.

taxationSystem.zh: 财政主要依赖进出口关税、港口费用、市场税、王室地产收益、附属港贡赋以及对黄金、象牙和奴隶贸易的抽成；农业直接税相对有限。
taxationSystem.en: Revenue depended mainly on import-export customs duties, harbor fees, market taxes, royal estate income, tribute from dependent ports, and levies on the gold, ivory, and slave trades; direct agricultural taxation was relatively limited.

religion.zh: 逊尼派伊斯兰教为城市主导宗教，清真寺、经学院与苏菲敬虔实践广泛存在；同时海岸社会仍保留部分非洲地方习俗，尤其在婚礼、祖先记忆与日常生活中。
religion.en: Sunni Islam was the dominant urban religion, with mosques, Quranic learning, and forms of Sufi piety widely present; at the same time, coastal society retained some African local customs, especially in weddings, ancestral memory, and everyday life.

philosophy.zh: 思想以伊斯兰伦理、商贸实用主义、家族名望观念和海洋世界观为主，重视信誉、契约、施舍、朝觐理想与与印度洋穆斯林共同体的联系。
philosophy.en: Thought was shaped by Islamic ethics, mercantile pragmatism, lineage prestige, and a maritime worldview, emphasizing trust, contract, charity, the ideal of pilgrimage, and connection to the wider Indian Ocean Muslim community.

writingSystem.zh: 主要书写系统为阿拉伯字母；阿拉伯文用于宗教、法律和外交，斯瓦希里语也常以阿拉伯字母书写于账目、诗歌和实用文书中。
writingSystem.en: The primary writing system was the Arabic script; Arabic was used for religion, law, and diplomacy, while Swahili was also commonly written in Arabic script for accounts, poetry, and practical documents.

culturalAchievements.zh: 斯瓦希里海岸以珊瑚石建筑、穹顶清真寺、雕刻门框、城市宅邸、航海知识、诗歌传统以及融合非洲、阿拉伯、波斯与印度洋元素的都市文化著称。基尔瓦大清真寺和胡苏尼库布瓦宫殿群体现了其建筑与商业文明的高度发展。
culturalAchievements.en: The Swahili Coast was noted for coral-stone architecture, domed mosques, carved doorways, urban residences, navigational knowledge, poetic traditions, and an urban culture blending African, Arab, Persian, and broader Indian Ocean elements. The Great Mosque of Kilwa and the palace complex of Husuni Kubwa exemplified the high development of its architecture and commercial civilization.

languageFamily.zh: 主体语言为班图语系的斯瓦希里语，并深受阿拉伯语影响；阿拉伯语在宗教与国际贸易中具有高地位。
languageFamily.en: The principal language was Swahili, a Bantu language heavily influenced by Arabic; Arabic held high prestige in religion and international trade.

unit.zh: 基尔瓦银第尔汗等值/人
unit.en: Kilwa silver dirham equivalent per person

gdpDescription.zh: 整体经济以海上转口贸易而非大规模农业税收为核心。尽管总人口不大，但由于控制东非黄金、象牙和奴隶向印度洋市场输出的关键节点，基尔瓦在15世纪末仍保持高于其人口规模的商业产值；然而到1500年前后，港口竞争加剧、金矿贸易路线波动与政治分裂已使其经济走向衰退。
gdpDescription.en: The economy was centered on maritime entrepôt trade rather than broad agrarian taxation. Although the population was not large, Kilwa still maintained commercial output disproportionate to its size in the late fifteenth century because it controlled key outlets for East African gold, ivory, and slaves into Indian Ocean markets; however, by around 1500, growing port competition, shifting gold routes, and political fragmentation were pushing it into decline.

mainIndustries.zh: 远洋贸易、港口转运、航运、造船与修船、珊瑚石建筑、渔业、椰枣与椰子制品、纺织品再分销、金银与珠饰加工、奴隶贸易
mainIndustries.en: Long-distance trade, port transshipment, shipping, boatbuilding and repair, coral-stone construction, fishing, date and coconut products, textile redistribution, gold and bead finishing, and the slave trade

tradeGoods.zh: 出口黄金、象牙、奴隶、玳瑁、木材、犀角、琥珀样香料与部分铁器；进口印度棉布、丝绸、瓷器、玻璃珠、金属器、香料、珠宝、马匹与奢侈消费品。
tradeGoods.en: Exports included gold, ivory, slaves, tortoiseshell, timber, rhinoceros horn, aromatic products, and some iron goods; imports included Indian cotton cloth, silk, porcelain, glass beads, metalware, spices, jewelry, horses, and luxury goods.

name.zh: 基尔瓦铜币与银第尔汗并用体系
name.en: Kilwa copper coinage and silver dirham system

householdWealth.zh: 沿海城市富商家庭拥有石屋、进口陶瓷、布匹储备、奴仆与海船股份，财富远高于普通渔民和内陆供货者。多数家庭财产有限，依赖季风贸易、手工业或小规模渔农结合维生。
householdWealth.en: Wealthy coastal merchant households possessed stone houses, imported ceramics, cloth reserves, servants or slaves, and shares in seagoing vessels, placing them far above ordinary fishermen and inland suppliers.
